title: BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT/LON hardcoded metropolitan poison filter rejects all non-Białystok coords
evidence: BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT = (52.6, 53.7); BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LON = (22.3, 24.1). Pure literals (no os.environ). coords_in_bialystok_bbox() returns False for anything outside; used in courier_resolver GPS sanity (l.215, l.912), OSRM coord guard (Lekcja #140), and as the superset the geocode bbox must be ⊂ of (invariant test_geo_bbox_consistency).
what_is_coupled: Every GPS fix, OSRM input coordinate and geocode result is validated against this box. In city #2 ALL legitimate coordinates fall outside (52.6-53.7,22.3-24.1) → GPS_BBOX_REJECT on every courier, OSRM coords treated as cross-country poison → no feasible fleet, dispatch fully dead. Hard blocker for even standing up a 2nd city.
proposed_config_key: city.service_metro_bbox = {lat:[min,max], lon:[min,max]} (must be a strict superset of geocode_service_bbox)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: draw a box around the new city center +~55km at launch; never learned

verification.reasoning:
Re-opened common.py:431-432: BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT=(52.6,53.7), BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LON=(22.3,24.1) are PURE LITERALS with no os.environ/getenv override. Grepped all env reads in common.py: the SIBLING GEOCODE_BBOX_* IS env-driven (GEOCODE_BBOX_LAT_MIN/MAX/LON read from os.environ at 907-910), but the metropolitan poison-filter bbox is deliberately a hard constant, so the 'already config/env' escape does NOT apply here. coords_in_bialystok_bbox() returns False for any coord outside the box.

Verified the filter is load-bearing across three decision surfaces, all of which FAIL CLOSED for city #2 coords: (1) osrm_client.py:560 + 646-647 - if from/to or matrix origins/dests are outside bbox, OSRM is NOT called and a sentinel 'invalid coord' result is returned (route = poison) so no feasible legs computable; (2) courier_resolver.py:215 - last-known-position rescue returns None when GPS is outside bbox, courier loses position; (3) dispatch_pipeline.py:2994-2996 - bag pickup/delivery coords outside bbox trigger re-geocode repair, and since a city-#2 address geocodes outside this box too, repair fails and coords fall back to (0,0), which OSRM then sentinels. Net effect in a 2nd city: every GPS fix rejected, every OSRM input sentineled, every bag coord repaired to (0,0), zero feasible fleet, dispatch fully dead. This blocks even STANDING UP city #2, so P0 stands.

This is NOT universal physics (it is a geographic literal tied to one metro), NOT shareable across cities (each city needs its own box), and NOT already-configurable (no override, unlike its geocode sibling). Proposed key city.service_metro_bbox={lat:[min,max],lon:[min,max]} is correct; must remain a strict superset of geocode_service_bbox per the test_geo_bbox_consistency invariant (tests/test_geo_bbox_consistency.py:42-44). Bootstrap is a COLD-START FIXED value: city center +/-~55km, hand-set at launch from the new city's known service area; no data/learning needed. Fix is small (mirror the env pattern already used for GEOCODE_BBOX_*) but its absence is fatal, hence P0 not lower.

title: BIALYSTOK_CENTER (53.1325,23.1688) synthetic no-GPS courier position used ~8x
evidence: BIALYSTOK_CENTER = (53.1325, 23.1688). Set as cs.pos for couriers without GPS/history (pre-shift, post-shift-synthetic, no_gps fallback); also bootstrap_restaurants re-geocode sanity d_center = haversine_km(coords, BIALYSTOK_CENTER). Literal, not config.
what_is_coupled: A no-GPS courier in city #2 is teleported to Białystok center (hundreds of km away). dispatch_pipeline launders km_to_pickup to fleet-average and travel_min to max(prep,15) which softens scoring, but the raw coordinate still flows into bbox checks and any geometry not laundered → degraded/incorrect feasibility for blind couriers. The bootstrap re-geocode sanity distance also references the wrong center.
proposed_config_key: city.center_coords = [lat, lon]
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: the new city's geometric/market center at launch

verification.reasoning: CONFIRMED hardcoded, NOT config/env: literal (53.1325,23.1688) appears in 3 copies — courier_resolver.py:110, bootstrap_restaurants.py:19, and dispatch_pipeline.py:132 (_BIALYSTOK_CENTER_FALLBACK). It is genuinely a per-city value (city centroid) that must change for city #2, so it is a real coupling — not already-configurable. HOWEVER the P1 severity is overstated because the claimed mechanism ('degraded/incorrect feasibility', 'raw coordinate flows into bbox / geometry not laundered') is largely neutralized: (1) dispatch_pipeline.py:5694-5719 overwrites the decision-relevant fields for pos_source=='no_gps' — km_to_pickup=fleet_avg_km (mean of REAL fleet positions, automatically city-local), travel_min/drive_min/eta_pickup_utc=max(15,prep_remaining). (2) R9 wait penalty uses effective_drive_min=max(15,prep) (dispatch_pipeline.py:4214-4216), not raw center geometry. (3) The bbox check coords_in_bialystok_bbox (courier_resolver.py:910, dispatch_pipeline.py:2994-2996) is applied to REAL GPS to reject junk; a synthetic centroid trivially passes its own city's bbox, so no harm flows there — and the bbox itself is a SEPARATE coupling, not this one. Residual REAL harm (why not NOT-A-BLOCKER): per the comment at dispatch_pipeline.py:5697 the raw SCORE is kept ('liczony z centrum') on the rationale center≈fleet-median; with equal-treatment (no_gps no longer demoted) a no-GPS courier's direction/proximity score is computed from the centroid — fine in Białystok, mis-ranked in city #2; and bootstrap_restaurants.validate() (line 163, OUTLIER_KM=15) would flag every city-#2 restaurant as an outlier. Both effects are confined to an edge subset (no-GPS + no recent history, a minority given courier-app GPS + last-known-pos store) and a one-time, log-only setup tool. Does NOT block standing up city #2 (not P0); degrades quality for a blind-courier edge case (P2), trivially fixed by one config key. CONFIG: city.center_coords=[lat,lon], replacing all 3 literals + feeding bootstrap OUTLIER_KM anchor. BOOTSTRAP: cold-start fixed value — read the new city's centroid off a map once at launch; no learning needed, no N-day calibration.

title: GEOCODE_BBOX_* service-area guard env-overridable but defaults to Białystok+28km
evidence: GEOCODE_BBOX_LAT_MIN/MAX = env.get default '52.85'/'53.35'; LON '22.85'/'23.45'; ENABLE_GEOCODE_BBOX_GUARD default '1'. geocoding._bbox_config() hard fallback (True,52.85,53.35,22.85,23.45) if import fails. Comment l.903: 'Multi-tenant Warsaw: bbox env-overridable per deploy.'
what_is_coupled: Accepts/rejects every geocode result before cache write (_in_service_bbox). Already per-tenant-ready via env, but the literal default and the import-fail hard fallback are Białystok. If a city #2 deploy forgets to set the env vars, every legit local geocode is rejected as out-of-bbox → no_pickup_geocode/KOORD. Must be set together with metro bbox honoring GEOCODE_BBOX ⊂ METRO_BBOX invariant.
proposed_config_key: city.geocode_service_bbox = {lat:[min,max], lon:[min,max]} (subset of service_metro_bbox); replace env-default + geocoding.py hard fallback with config
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: service area box at launch; keep the subset invariant vs metro bbox

verification.reasoning: The bbox values are NOT hardcoded literals — common.py:906-910 read all five via os.environ.get() with Białystok defaults, and common.py:903 carries an explicit "Multi-tenant Warsaw: bbox env-overridable per deploy" annotation. The mechanism to make this per-city already exists and is intentional. The geocoding.py:152 hard fallback (True,52.85,53.35,22.85,23.45) is reached only on import failure of common (an exception path), not during normal operation where the env-driven values flow through. So the "hardcoded-value / P1" framing is overstated: this is single-tenant-assumption only in the DEFAULT, with a fully-supported per-deploy override. It does NOT block standing up city #2 (set 5 env vars), and it's not a scale/quality blocker. I downgrade from P1 to P2 rather than NOT-A-BLOCKER for two real reasons: (1) it is a MANDATORY launch-config item — if a city #2 deploy forgets the env vars, every legit local geocode is rejected as out-of-bbox → no_pickup_geocode/KOORD storm, i.e. catastrophic-but-trivially-fixable, exactly a launch-checklist P2; (2) the geocoding.py Białystok fallback should be neutralized/parameterized so the degenerate path isn't silently city-specific. Recommend lifting the 5 env vars into a city config object (city.geocode_service_bbox) with a launch-time invariant check GEOCODE_BBOX ⊂ METRO_BBOX, and removing the literal fallback in favor of fail-loud. Cold-start bootstrap: fixed value (metro bounding box drawn around the service area on day 0), no learning needed.

title: address_mismatch validator hardwires 'bialystok' as the privileged home town
evidence: _street_name_key strips leading 'białystok '/'bialystok ' (l.48-49); city default v.get('city') or 'Białystok' (l.64); if city.startswith('bialystok') → city='bialystok' (l.65-66); check_street_town: tk.startswith('bialystok') → return None 'bez zastrzeżeń' (l.90-91); suggest_town: 'Białystok' (l.102). The distribution logic itself is data-driven from geocode_cache, but the home-town reference is the literal.
what_is_coupled: The street↔town mismatch warning treats Białystok as the always-correct home city; any other town is flagged as suspicious and the system suggests rewriting to 'Białystok'. In city #2 every legitimate local address would be flagged and 'corrected' to Białystok. Advisory layer (shadow flag ENABLE_ADDRESS_TOWN_MISMATCH_SHADOW) so it degrades quality rather than blocking dispatch.
proposed_config_key: city.home_town_key = 'bialystok' (lowercased canonical home-town string) + city.home_town_display
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: the new city's own canonical town key/name

verification.reasoning:
Hardcoding is REAL, not config/env-driven. Re-read address_mismatch.py confirms every cited literal: l.48 _street_name_key strips 'bialystok '/'bialystok ' prefix; l.64 _town_key(v.get('city') or 'Bialystok'); l.65-66 if city.startswith('bialystok'): city='bialystok'; l.90-91 check_street_town short-circuits to None when tk.startswith('bialystok'); l.102 suggest_town='Bialystok'. Thresholds (_ADDR_CHECK_MIN_BIA=5, _ADDR_CHECK_MAX_HERE=1) and the street distribution ARE data-driven from geocode_cache, but the privileged home-town string is a literal constant; none read from env or a config object.

Genuinely single-tenant, NOT universal physics: two cities cannot share a 'home town' string. Bialystok is structurally the always-correct reference and every other town is flagged suspicious and suggested for rewrite. So the coupling is confirmed and the proposed config key (city.home_town_key + city.home_town_display) is the correct replacement.

Severity P2 is accurate (not overstated, not refuted). ENGINE side is shadow/log-only: gated by ENABLE_ADDRESS_TOWN_MISMATCH_SHADOW (true in flags.json:223), wired only at shadow_dispatcher.py:1153-1157 via maybe_log_mismatch which only appends to dispatch_state/address_mismatch_shadow.jsonl and never alters a dispatch decision. In city #2 the engine side would emit false-mismatch noise, not block dispatch (could even be left flag-off). BUT the twin is LIVE and user-facing: nadajesz_clone/panel/backend/app/api/dispatch.py:503-526 is identical hardcoding, exposed at REST GET /address-check (l.529-537) and called inline at l.345; its warning text (l.520-522) and suggest_town (l.522) literally say 'Bialymstoku'/'Bialystok'. In city #2 every legit local street would be flagged 'occurs mainly in Bialystok, check the town' and pushed to rewrite to Bialystok, actively misleading waitstaff/coordinators. That is real quality degradation but it does not block standing up city #2 (not P0) nor block scale fundamentally (not P1), and it is more than cosmetic (not P3) because it produces wrong, action-triggering suggestions. Hence P2 stands.

Completeness note for the launch team: this is a TWIN-path class. Both address_mismatch.py (engine shadow) AND panel app/api/dispatch.py (live) carry the same literal and the same MIN_BIA=5/MAX_HERE=1 thresholds, and BOTH depend on a geocode_cache whose town distribution is itself currently single-tenant (mixing two cities into one global cache would also corrupt the 'strongly-Bialystok' statistic). A complete per-city fix replaces the literal with city.home_town_key/home_town_display in both files and scopes the geocode_cache per city. Bootstrap: cold-start fixed value only (set the new city's canonical town string in config); no learning window needed for the home-town string itself, though the distribution stats need the city's own geocode_cache to accumulate before the warning is meaningful.

title: districts_data.py (1376 lines, 28 Białystok osiedla) + BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY power bundling/po-drodze/geocode-verification
evidence: BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS = {28 osiedla from info.bialystok.pl, street frozensets} (districts_data.py); BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY = {...} (common.py:1298) consumed by neighbor lookup (l.1563) for bundle/po-drodze compatibility; DistrictReverseLookup builds a kd-tree over the geocoded address_cache to map coord→district, returns 'Unknown' on failure.
what_is_coupled: District identity feeds same-/adjacent-district bundle penalties, 'po drodze' grouping and geocode district-verification (the local-coordinator MOAT quality). City #2 has no street→district map and no adjacency graph → every lookup falls to 'Unknown', collapsing district-aware bundling and verification to a degraded geometric-only mode. Not a hard dispatch stop (fallbacks exist) but a major quality regression — exactly the veteran-local-knowledge value being franchised.
proposed_config_key: city.districts_file (per-city street→district registry) + city.district_adjacency (neighbor graph); district_reverse_lookup auto-builds from that city's geocode_cache
bootstrap_note: external setup: fetch the new city's official osiedla/streets (city site or OSM) at launch; adjacency hand-drawn or derived; reverse-lookup kd-tree then improves as geocode_cache fills over days

verification.reasoning:
Coupling is real and hardcoded: BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS (districts_data.py:11) is a literal dict of 28 Białystok osiedla as street frozensets ("Source: info.bialystok.pl", "NIE edit manually"), and BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY (common.py:1298) is a hand-curated literal neighbor graph ("Final adjacency per ACK właściciela", with manual GEO-05 fix). Neither has any config/env indirection or per-tenant annotation — both imported as module constants. 100% city-locked.

Blocker level is P1, not the un-leveled "P1 claimed" overstated to standup-blocking: the fallback is graceful at three layers. district_reverse_lookup.py:9 returns 'Unknown' on any init/cache failure; drop_proximity_factor (common.py:1557-1560) maps Unknown/empty → 0.0 (no crash); and the value is consumed only as a SOFT multiplier on the same-restaurant bundle bonus (common.py:1278) and Bug-Z cross-quadrant penalty (min_drop_proximity_factor, common.py:3138). Hard feasibility (R6 35min, R1/R5 km spreads, corridor, same_restaurant_grouper) is OSRM-distance based and works without districts. So city #2 DOES stand up and dispatch in geometric-only mode — not P0. But it loses district-aware bundle credit, po-drodze refinement, and geocode_verify district cross-check (geocode_verify.py:10) — a genuine MOAT/quality regression, which is exactly the franchise value being scaled. Hence P1 (blocks quality/scale parity), not P2 cosmetic.

Correction to the finding's bootstrap claim: DistrictReverseLookup does NOT auto-recover from city #2's geocode_cache alone — its kd-tree calls drop_zone_from_address (geocoding.py:327) which still resolves against BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS street keys, so the per-city street→district registry must exist first. Also note address_mismatch.py:55-75 (street↔town from geocode_cache) is a SEPARATE, data-learned defense that generalizes per-city and should not be conflated with this registry.

Three reasons it never blocks city #2: (1) It only fires for orders whose address_id is in FIRMOWE_KONTO_ADDRESS_IDS = frozenset({161}) (common.py:3305), which is that tenant's specific corporate account; a new city tenant config would not contain 161, so the gate _aid_int in C.FIRMOWE_KONTO_ADDRESS_IDS (panel_watcher.py:1167, czasowka_scheduler.py:281) never matches and the fallback branch is unreachable. (3) It is per-tenant corporate-account DATA, not per-city physics, and the proposed config key (tenant.corporate_accounts[] = {address_id, fallback_coords}, optional/empty for a new city) is the right and trivial fix. evidence: BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS = {'Antoniuk': {'streets': frozenset({'antoniukowska','broniewskiego władysława',...})}, ... } — 28 'streets': frozenset entries (grep count=28), module-level literal. Docstring: 'BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS data — 28 official osiedli z info.bialystok.pl. Source: http://www.info.bialystok.pl/osiedla/{1..28}/obiekt.php (2026-04-21 fetched). NIE edit manually — regen z oficjalnego źródła per process.' No env/config indirection (grep for DISTRICTS_DATA_PATH/districts.json = none).
what_is_coupled: Every street→district resolution is Białystok-only. A 2nd city has zero district coverage → drop_zone_from_address returns 'Unknown' for all its addresses → bundling colocation, corridor/trajectory scoring, drop_proximity bonus, and the LGBM district feature all silently degrade to neutral. Engine runs but dispatches like a tourist, not a veteran.
proposed_config_key: per-city districts dataset file: config/cities/<city>/districts.json (loaded by a CityGeo registry keyed on tenant id) replacing the module-level BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS constant
bootstrap_note: external setup: scrape the city's official osiedla registry (analog of info.bialystok.pl) OR derive from OSM admin_level=10 boundaries + reverse-geocode street centroids; then a 7-14 day shadow run to fill long-tail streets from observed geocode_cache (mirrors existing V3.28-DISTRICTS-LONG-TAIL backlog). Cannot be cold-started from a fixed value.

verification.reasoning:
The table is genuinely hardcoded with no config/env indirection. districts_data.py:11 defines BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS as a module-level literal of 28 streets-frozenset entries; docstring (lines 1-8) says "NIE edit manually - regen z oficjalnego zrodla" - i.e. the regenerate path produces ANOTHER hardcoded Python module (build_v319h_districts_data), not a loadable per-city data file. grep for DISTRICTS_DATA_PATH / districts.json returns nothing. So looks-hardcoded == is-hardcoded is verified.

The coupling is actually BROADER than the streets table: the same module also hardcodes BIALYSTOK_OUTSIDE_CITY_ZONES (districts_data.py:1290), QUADRANT_BY_DISTRICT (1302), and adjacency/classify_trajectory (1338) - all Bialystok-specific. common.py:1437 drop_zone_from_address hardcodes the branch city_lc != 'bialystok' and only matches against BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS, so for any 2nd city every address that is not an explicit outside-city zone returns 'Unknown'. coupling_type hardcoded-value is correct; genuinely per-city, NOT universal physics and NOT shareable between cities (street to district maps are intrinsically local).

Why NOT P0 (refuting over-escalation): degradation is graceful, verified at dispatch_pipeline.py:3591-3599 - the Z-02 sign-guard treats an 'Unknown' zone as a SOFT bundle multiplier (V327_BUNDLE_UNKNOWN_SCORE_MULT ~0.7), NOT the harsh 0.1 cross-quadrant penalty and NOT a hard-reject. So a 2nd-city engine STANDS UP and dispatches; it just loses corridor/trajectory/colocation/drop-proximity geo intelligence and the LGBM district feature collapses to neutral. That is exactly the veteran-local-coordinator quality that is the franchise MOAT, so it blocks scale/quality not standup -> P1 is correct (matches the prior auditor; not overstated, not refuted).

Note: the city NAME is already env-driven (dispatch_pipeline.py:60 DEFAULT_CITY=os.environ.get('ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY','Bialystok')) - but that only renames the default, it does NOT make the geo table per-tenant. Proposed config key config/cities/<city>/districts.json is sound but must bundle the full geo set (streets + outside-city zones + quadrant map + adjacency), loaded by a CityGeo registry keyed on tenant. bootstrap_note: cold-start = scrape the new city's official osiedle/district source into districts.json + hand-author quadrant/adjacency (1-2 day analyst task); no learned-from-data phase needed since it is static municipal data.

title: drop_zone_from_address hardcodes literal 'białystok' as the home-city sentinel and ignores ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY
evidence: def drop_zone_from_address(addr, city=None): ... if city_norm and city_lc != 'białystok': # Outside-city — detect explicit zones ... The string 'białystok' is a literal branch condition. dispatch_pipeline.py:60 defines DEFAULT_CITY = os.environ.get('ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY', 'Białystok') but drop_zone_from_address never reads it.
what_is_coupled: Any non-Białystok city name on an order is treated as 'outside city' and only matched against the 6-element OUTSIDE_CITY_ZONES; the home-city street table is keyed to the literal. In city #2, every local delivery would be classed outside-city → 'Unknown'. Also an internal inconsistency: a partial config knob (ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY) exists but this function bypasses it.
proposed_config_key: city.home_name (canonical lowercased home-city string) consumed by drop_zone_from_address instead of the 'białystok' literal; wire it to the same source as ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: set per city at launch (the city's own name). No data needed.

verification.reasoning:
The hardcoding is real. common.py:1449 branches on a bare literal city_lc != 'białystok', and common.py contains NO reference to DEFAULT_CITY (grep returned NONE) — the env knob ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY is defined ONLY in dispatch_pipeline.py:60 and is never imported/consumed by drop_zone_from_address. The internal-inconsistency claim is correct. Hot-path callers dispatch_pipeline.py:3576 and :3962 pass order_event.get('delivery_city') (the order's actual city string), so in city #2 every local delivery (delivery_city='Warszawa') hits != 'białystok' -> outside-city branch -> matched only against BIALYSTOK_OUTSIDE_CITY_ZONES -> 'Unknown' for ALL local drops. Confirmed.

Why P1 (not P0, not P2): the output feeds SOFT scoring only — the Bug-Z cross-quadrant bundle multiplier (3574-3600) and the V319h same-restaurant drop-proximity factor (3958-3984). It NEVER gates HARD feasibility; real distances come from OSRM coords, so dispatch still stands up and runs in city #2 (rules out P0). But it is more than cosmetic: drop_proximity_factor (common.py:1559) returns 0.0 for any 'Unknown' zone, zeroing legitimate same-restaurant L1 bundle bonuses -> Ziomek loses district-aware bundling discrimination, which is core to the 'veteran local coordinator' value (rules out P2 degrades-only). Existing defensive code (Z-02 sign guard, V327_BUNDLE_UNKNOWN_SCORE_MULT=0.7 at 3593-3597) makes the cross-quadrant side fail GRACEFULLY rather than punitively, so it is a quality blocker, not a crash.

Caveat: fixing the literal is NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT. Even with city.home_name wired correctly, the home-city branch matches addresses against BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS (the ~40KB districts_data.py table, 100% Białystok streets), so city #2 still returns 'Unknown' until it ships its own district/street table. This literal is the gate; the per-city district table is the bulk. Config key city.home_name (lowercased) should replace the literal AND be wired from the same source as ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY; bootstrap = cold-start fixed value set at city onboarding, paired with a per-city districts table.

title: District adjacency graph manually curated in common.py, drives corridor/bundle compatibility
evidence: BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY = {'Centrum': {'Przydworcowe','Piaski','Bojary',...}, ...} hand-curated neighbor sets. Inline comment 'GEO-05 fix 2026-06-13: usunięto Dojlidy Górne (centroidy 4.38 km, Dojlidy leży MIĘDZY nimi... fałszywe sąsiedztwo)' shows it is tuned from centroid distance measurements + Adrian domain knowledge.
what_is_coupled: are_orders_groupable (same_restaurant_grouper) and drop_proximity_factor / classify_trajectory use this map to decide if two drops are 'compatible' (adjacent) for bundling and corridor bonuses. Wrong/empty map for city #2 → either no bundling (all cross-quadrant penalty) or bad bundling. This is the veteran-coordinator 'which neighborhoods chain well' knowledge.
proposed_config_key: config/cities/<city>/district_adjacency.json (dict district→neighbors), injected as the adjacency_map argument already accepted by grouper/classifier
bootstrap_note: learned-from-data over N days: auto-derive an initial graph from district centroid pairwise distance (link if < threshold AND no third district between, per the GEO-05 method), then refine from observed successful bundles. Cold-start fallback = centroid-distance threshold graph.

verification.reasoning: Re-opened common.py:1298-1360. BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY is a pure module-level literal dict (62 zone→neighbor sets), with the GEO-05 inline comment confirming it was hand-tuned from centroid distances + Adrian domain knowledge. Grepped for env/config/JSON override: NONE exists (no os.environ/json/load assignment; only direct references at common.py:1563, geocoding.py:309, dispatch_pipeline.py:894/943, route_simulator_v2.py:312, ml_inference.py:606). So 'is hardcoded' is confirmed, not assumed. NOT refuted and NOT already-configurable: although the consumer functions (are_orders_groupable, group_orders_by_restaurant, classify_trajectory) accept adjacency_map as a parameter (injection plumbing already exists), every call site hardwires C.BIALYSTOK_DISTRICT_ADJACENCY, so per-city swap currently requires a code edit, not config. Severity downgraded from any P0 reading: it is a SOFT scoring factor, not a HARD gate. are_orders_groupable returns True for same-district drops (q1==q2, line 95) INDEPENDENT of the map; only cross-district-but-adjacent grouping and the corridor relation consult it, feeding bonuses V326_R06_BONUS_SAME=40 / BONUS_SIMILAR=15 / PENALTY_OPPOSITE=-40. An empty/wrong map for city #2 therefore degrades gracefully (no crash, same-district bundling preserved, cross-district bundling lost, corridor +/- mis-fires) — so it does NOT block STANDING UP a 2nd city (not P0). It merits P1 (not P2) because cross-district bundling + corridor classification is precisely the 'veteran local coordinator knows which neighborhoods chain' knowledge that is the franchise value proposition, and it cannot be auto-derived to MOAT quality without local curation (the map required a GEO-05 correction iteration removing a false 4.38km adjacency). Mitigating: it is a downstream LEAF of the heavier geo coupling (districts_data.py street→district resolver + centroids), and eod_drafts/2026-05-05/_adjacency_compute.py already auto-computes centroid adjacency, so once districts_data exists for a city the adjacency is semi-derivable and the injection refactor is small. Proposed config key: config/cities/<city>/district_adjacency.json (dict district→neighbor list), loaded at startup and passed through the existing adjacency_map params; cold-start = auto-generate from centroid distances (<~2.5km threshold) via _adjacency_compute.py then have local ops review/prune false links (Adrian-style ACK), refine over 14-30 days from observed bundle outcomes. coupling_type is more accurately single-tenant-assumption/hardcoded-value (hand-curated literal) with a calibrated-from-data centroid input, rather than purely calibrated-from-data.

title: QUADRANT_BY_DISTRICT 6-bucket compass + _OPPOSITE_QUADRANT_PAIRS are Białystok-geometry specific
evidence: QUADRANT_BY_DISTRICT = {'Antoniuk':'N','Bacieczki':'N',...,'Bema':'SW','Centrum':'CENTER',...} with comment 'Adrian ACK 2026-04-23 23:30: Wasilków → E, S rozbite na SE/SW'. _OPPOSITE_QUADRANT_PAIRS = frozenset({frozenset({'N','SE'}),frozenset({'N','SW'}),frozenset({'E','W'})}).
what_is_coupled: classify_trajectory (districts_data.py:1338) buckets every district into a compass octant to decide OPPOSITE (cross-town) vs SIDEWAYS, feeding cross-quadrant bundle penalties (V327_BUNDLE_CROSS_QUADRANT_SCORE_MULT=0.1 in common.py). Per-city: which districts are 'north', and which octant-pairs count as 'opposite' (cross-city) depends entirely on the city's shape and where Centrum sits. The 6-bucket model + opposite-pairs may not even fit a differently-shaped city.
proposed_config_key: config/cities/<city>/quadrant_map.json (district→octant) + opposite_pairs list; OR replace octant heuristic with direct centroid-bearing computation per city
bootstrap_note: learned-from-data: compute each district centroid, assign octant by bearing from city center; opposite-pairs derive from ~180° bearing difference. Cold-start = auto bearing assignment from centroids (no manual ACK needed if centroids known).

verification.reasoning: Re-read districts_data.py:1302-1382: QUADRANT_BY_DISTRICT is a literal module-level dict of ~35 Bialystok district names to 6 compass octants (N/E/SE/SW/W/CENTER) with the cited 'Adrian ACK 2026-04-23' comment; _OPPOSITE_QUADRANT_PAIRS is a literal frozenset of N-SE, N-SW, E-W. Neither reads env/config, so it is genuinely hardcoded, not already config-driven. classify_trajectory() consumes them to bucket districts OPPOSITE (cross-town) vs SIDEWAYS/SIMILAR, feeding R-06 trajectory penalties (common.py:2164-2168: PENALTY_OPPOSITE=-40, PENALTY_SIDEWAYS=-10, BONUS_SAME=+40) and the bundle cross-quadrant multiplier V327_BUNDLE_CROSS_QUADRANT_SCORE_MULT=0.1 (common.py:3092,3110-3137). District names and octant assignment are entirely Bialystok-specific; a new city's districts are absent from the dict, so the map is useless and unshareable across cities. NOT P0 due to defensive fallbacks: unmapped districts return UNKNOWN and bundle_score_multiplier(None) returns SAME_QUADRANT_MULT=1.0 (no penalty), so city #2 still boots and dispatches, it just silently loses cross-town trajectory/bundle intelligence and degrades the veteran-coordinator do-not-bundle-across-town moat. That is quality/scale degradation = P1, matching the prior. Coupling-type correction: prior labeled calibrated-from-data, but it is a hand-curated ACK'd map = single-tenant-assumption/hardcoded-value, not learned. Replacement: config/cities/<city>/quadrant_map.json OR centroid-bearing computation from per-city district centroids (cold-start derivable day 1, no learning window).

title: Street-town mismatch detector hardcodes 'bialystok' as home city in 4 spots (+ panel twin)
evidence: city = _town_key(v.get('city') or 'Białystok'); if city.startswith('bialystok'): city='bialystok'. check_street_town: if tk.startswith('bialystok'): return None # Białystok = bez zastrzeżeń. Verdict dict hardcodes 'suggest_town':'Białystok'. Thresholds _ADDR_CHECK_MIN_BIA=5/_ADDR_CHECK_MAX_HERE=1. Docstring flags a TWIN: panel app/api/dispatch.py:check_street_town with identical thresholds — 'Zmiana progu = zmień OBA miejsca'.
what_is_coupled: The 'this street is strongly a HOME-CITY street so the foreign town label is probably wrong' heuristic is anchored to the literal 'bialystok'. In city #2 it would never fire for local streets and would falsely suggest 'Białystok' as the correction. Twin in panel must change in lockstep.
proposed_config_key: city.home_name (reuse same key as geo-drop-zone) consumed by both _street_town_counts default and check_street_town home-skip + suggest_town; thresholds MIN_BIA/MAX_HERE can stay global
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed (city name) for the literal; the count distribution self-populates from that city's geocode_cache over time (the detector needs ~N days of cache before it has signal anyway).

verification.reasoning:
Hardcoding is REAL and confirmed in both twins; not config/env-driven. In address_mismatch.py the home city is a bare literal in 4 spots: line 64 _town_key(v.get('city') or 'Bialystok') (default-city fill for cache rows), lines 65-66 if city.startswith('bialystok'): city='bialystok' (bucket collapse), lines 90-91 if tk.startswith('bialystok'): return None (home-skip), line 102 'suggest_town':'Bialystok' (correction label). Thresholds lines 27-28 MIN_BIA=5/MAX_HERE=1. grep for os.environ/getenv/C.flag/config/HOME_CITY in the file returns nothing (exit 1) — the ENABLE_ADDRESS_TOWN_MISMATCH_SHADOW flag gates only the CALLER (shadow_dispatcher.py:1153), not the city name. The TWIN is independently hardcoded: panel backend app/api/dispatch.py lines 405-407, 482-484, 509-510, 522 carry byte-identical literals plus thresholds 449-450, so the docstring 'zmien OBA miejsca' lockstep warning is accurate.

But P2 (degrades) is the correct level and the finding's failure-mode is partly overstated. Trace for city #2 (Warszawa): check_street_town computes bia=counts.get('bialystok',0) and the fire gate needs bia>=5. In an ISOLATED per-tenant geocode_cache (Warsaw-only) there is no 'bialystok' bucket, so bia=0 and the detector NEVER fires — it is silently INERT (loses the typo-catching safety net), NOT actively suggesting Bialystok for local streets as the finding asserts. The 'would falsely suggest Bialystok' harm only materializes under a SHARED global geocode_cache (today dispatch_state/ is single-tenant/global): then a common Polish street present in both cities (Armii Krajowej, Wiejska, Lesna, Mickiewicza — ubiquitous) would have bia>=5 from Bialystok rows and fire a wrong 'did you mean Bialystok?' hint on a legit Warsaw order.

Net: confirmed real per-city coupling needing a config key, NOT already-configurable, NOT universal physics. Both surfaces are non-decisional — engine side is shadow/log-only (maybe_log_mismatch writes jsonl, does not alter dispatch), panel side is a /estimate UX warning a coordinator can ignore — so it cannot block STANDING UP city #2 (not P0) nor hard-block scale (not P1). Worst case is a stale safety net or a mild dismissable false-positive hint under shared cache = P2, as originally classified. Fix: replace literals with city.home_name (reuse the geo-drop-zone key) consumed by _street_town_counts default-city, check_street_town home-skip, and suggest_town; keep MIN_BIA/MAX_HERE global (universal statistical thresholds). Bootstrap: cold-start fixed value = the new city's display name (e.g. 'Warszawa') set at tenant onboarding; no learning needed. Must land in lockstep with the panel twin (dispatch.py).

title: District reverse-lookup is a global singleton over one hardcoded Białystok address_cache path
evidence: ADDRESS_CACHE_PATH = '/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/ml_data_prep/data/address_cache.json'. _instance = None ... def get_district_lookup(): global _instance; if _instance is None: _instance = DistrictReverseLookup(). The kd-tree is built from whatever geocoded addresses are in that one file (Białystok), and the singleton is process-global; lookup delegates to drop_zone_from_address which is itself Białystok-keyed.
what_is_coupled: coord→district resolution for LGBM features depends on a single global kd-tree built from one city's address cache, with no tenant key. Running two cities in one process is impossible (one singleton, one path). Even per-process, the path and the underlying drop_zone tables are Białystok.
proposed_config_key: per-city address_cache path + a tenant-keyed registry (dict[city_id]->DistrictReverseLookup) instead of a single module global; path from config/cities/<city>/paths.address_cache
bootstrap_note: learned-from-data: the address_cache fills as the city geocodes its orders; until populated the lookup correctly returns 'Unknown' (defense-in-depth already present). Architecture change (namespacing the singleton) is the real work, not the data.

verification.reasoning: All mechanical claims verified TRUE: ADDRESS_CACHE_PATH is a literal hardcoded string (district_reverse_lookup.py:21), get_district_lookup() is a process-global singleton with no tenant key (:126-131), and lookup() delegates to drop_zone_from_address which is hardwired to BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS and returns 'Unknown' for any city other than bialystok (common.py:1437-1454). So this IS genuine single-tenant Bialystok coupling, not refuted. However P1 is overstated for four reasons. (1) The path is already half-parameterized: __init__ accepts address_cache_path (line 30); only the singleton default binds Bialystok. (2) Graceful degradation -- defense-in-depth (lines 9/82/91) returns 'Unknown' on missing cache, so a new city does not crash; it loses a feature, it does not block standing up city #2. (3) Both consumers are non-decisional today: geocoding.py:330 is fail-soft and gated by ENABLE_GEOCODE_VERIFICATION; ml_inference.py:617 is LGBM feature-engineering with ENABLE_LGBM_PRIMARY OFF (common.py:2217) and a two-model shadow that flags.json:200 explicitly states is NOT consumed by the verdict. The district lookup is not on the live scoring path. (4) The deeper Bialystok coupling actually lives in common.py BIALYSTOK_DISTRICTS / drop_zone_from_address -- the singleton merely delegates, so fixing only the kd-tree path still returns 'Unknown' for city #2; this finding partially mislocates the blocker. verification.reasoning:
Hardcoding CONFIRMED (not refuted): common.py:455-461 FALLBACK_BASE_SPEEDS_KMH is a plain literal dict with comment 'peak korkowy Bialegostoku'; get_time_bucket (common.py:464-486) hardcodes Bialystok peak hours (Pn-Pt 15-19 rush, 11-15 lunch) as literal if-branches. Neither reads os.environ - verified by grep: ~30 neighboring constants in common.py (MAX_PICKUP_REACH_KM:351, BAG_TIME_DANGER_MIN:698, GEOCODE_BBOX_*:907) ARE env-overridable, these two are NOT. Companion HAVERSINE_ROAD_FACTOR_BIALYSTOK=1.37 (common.py:405) is also a bare literal. So 'is hardcoded' and 'is Bialystok-coupled' both hold. coupling_type: base speeds = calibrated-from-data; time buckets = single-tenant-assumption.

WHY P1 IS OVERSTATED -> P2: This is a FALLBACK-ONLY path. Tracing all consumers (grep): get_fallback_speed_kmh / get_time_bucket feed ONLY (1) _haversine_fallback (osrm_client.py:374), fires only when OSRM unreachable/circuit-open; (2) dispatch_pipeline.py:3905 drive estimate used only on OSRM-miss (comment at 3891 confirms OSRM is the single source of truth for ETA); (3) dispatch_pipeline.py:1567 post-shift-skip naive prefilter. The PRIMARY ETA path is OSRM, inherently per-city already (self-hosted regional routing server - a new city stands up its own OSRM). The SEPARATE V326_OSRM_TRAFFIC_TABLE owns live traffic-multiplier buckets; get_time_bucket here is NOT on the demand/primary path.

Therefore: does NOT block standing up city #2 (not P0) - with OSRM healthy these are never read. Does NOT block primary-path scale/quality (not P1) - it only degrades ETAs DURING OSRM degradation plus a minor prefilter. Cold-start generic urban values (20-32 km/h) are a reasonable mid-size-European-city default acceptable for launch; two similar Polish cities could even safely share them. Real coupling worth turning into config, but severity is 'degrades', i.e. P2, not P1.

PROPOSED CONFIG: traffic.fallback_base_speeds_kmh (per bucket) + traffic.time_buckets (hour ranges) + traffic.haversine_road_factor. Bootstrap: cold-start ship the current Bialystok values as the universal default (acceptable since fallback-only); after ~30 days of OSRM-vs-actual leg data per city, recalibrate base speeds via the anchor-A method already used for Bialystok and set the rush/lunch hour windows from the city's observed congestion profile.

A fresh city stands up with no maps → None → no effect. So it cannot block standing up (not P0) nor degrade live dispatch (not P2) — it is calibration enrichment that a city opts into after collecting data.

title: OSRM route cache TTL assumes Białystok's finite, fully-warmed route set
evidence: osrm_client.py:44 `CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 60*60 # ... Białystok skończony zestaw routes, 99% hit po 1h warm-up`; osrm_client.py:451 comment 'Białystok = skończony zbiór par'; TABLE_CACHE_MAX_SIZE=50000.
what_is_coupled: Cache sizing/TTL rationale assumes a small finite set of pickup↔drop pairs (small city). A larger city has more unique pairs → lower hit rate, possible cache pressure. The cache itself is process-global (single tenant) — sharing one cache across cities would collide keys (lat/lon rounding) though keys are coordinate-based so collisions are unlikely; main risk is per-process single OSRM target.
proposed_config_key: per-city `osrm.cache_max_size` / `osrm.cache_ttl_s` (and per-city process/instance, see osrm-extract finding)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: keep 1h TTL / 50k cells; raise for larger cities. No learning needed — operational tuning only.

verification.reasoning: Re-read osrm_client.py:44-45 and :457: CACHE_TTL_SECONDS (3600s), CACHE_MAX_SIZE (5000), TABLE_CACHE_MAX_SIZE (50000) ARE hardcoded literals, not env/config-driven (grep confirms only ENABLE_OSRM_TABLE_CELL_CACHE at :463 is flag-gated — a toggle, not the sizes/TTL). So "hardcoded" is true. BUT the blocker framing is overstated on two counts. (1) Coupling type: the Białystok comments at :44/:451 are rationale-only; the cache code itself (_cache_get/_set :430/:436, _table_cache :468/:479) is fully city-agnostic — it is a hardcoded performance-tuning value, not a load-bearing single-tenant assumption. (2) Risk: correctness is unaffected — cache is a pure optimization with time-based TTL expiry and graceful LRU eviction (drop oldest 10% at MAX_SIZE). City #2 runs its own process (flags.json/dispatch_state/panel are single-tenant globals per the audit anchors) with its own in-memory cache pointed at its own OSRM instance, so the "cross-city key collision" the finding raises is impossible (and the finding self-admits collisions are unlikely). Worst case for a bigger city = lower hit rate + more OSRM HTTP calls = marginally higher latency, which the eviction self-manages; the 1h TTL is fine for any city. Does NOT block standing up a 2nd city and barely degrades quality — P3 (a nice-to-have per-city tuning knob), leaning NOT-A-BLOCKER for standup. Proposed keys osrm.cache_ttl_s / osrm.cache_max_size / osrm.table_cache_max_size remain valid as cold-start fixed defaults (current values), optionally retuned upward from data once a larger city's unique-pair count is observed.

Live courier_tiers.json _meta carries 24 cid->tier owner judgements ("last_panel_tier_edit 2026-06-26: cid 21 -> gold"). These are literal cid integers of named Białystok drivers, not config.
what_is_coupled: The seed/authoritative tier of every gold/std+/slow courier is a literal cid of a specific Białystok person. In another city these cids either don't exist or belong to different people, so the seed is meaningless and (worse) could mislabel a stranger. Gabriel's cap=4 'quality drops above 4' is an individual's documented limit.
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/courier_tiers.json + per_city/<city>/tier_ground_truth_seed.json (cid->tier owner overrides), empty for a new city
bootstrap_note: cold-start: empty seed -> everyone defaults to 'std' (TIER_GROUND_TRUTH.get(cid,'std')) and new hires get tier='new'; learned-from-data: after ~30-60 days courier_reliability.py throughput + panel ranking promote couriers to gold/std+/slow. Owner can hand-seed a few known stars day-1 via panel edit.

verification.reasoning: The named-cid constants ARE real: build_v319h_courier_tiers.py:32-52 has TIER_GROUND_TRUTH = {"123":"gold"#Bartek O,"413":"gold","179":"gold"[cap=4],"400":"std+","504":"slow"...} and CAP_OVERRIDE={"179":{peak:4,...,"quality drops above 4 - owner note 2026-04-20"}}, and live courier_tiers.json _meta.tier_ground_truth_cids carries 24 owner judgements with last_panel_tier_edit. But the finding overstates them as "the authoritative tier of every courier." (1) The runtime authoritative source is the JSON DATA file, panel/r04-editable, NOT code: courier_resolver.py:860 reads cap from _bag_info.get("cap_override") (the JSON, not the constant); _meta shows last_panel_tier_edit + last_r04_apply, and r04_apply.py atomic-writes courier_tiers.json from the learning pipeline. build_v319h is a self-described one-shot regenerator ("Jednorazowe narzedzie"); grep confirms no runtime module imports TIER_GROUND_TRUTH/CAP_OVERRIDE (only an eod_drafts regen script references it). So a new city is not configured by editing these constants - tiers live as data. (2) It does NOT block standing up city 2: build_v319h line 44 defaults every unknown cid to "std", so a fresh city's couriers all run at std and dispatch functions - not P0/P1-for-standup. The cost is QUALITY: gold/slow refinement and Gabriel's cap=4 are part of the veteran-coordinator moat, absent until per-city tier data is built. That is degradation, not a scale-blocker -> P2. (3) The dangerous part the finding raises - a stranger in city 2 inheriting cid 179's gold/cap=4 - is caused not by these constants but by courier_tiers.json being a single GLOBAL file under dispatch_state/ (the already-tracked global-single-tenant tenancy anchor). Fixing that one tenancy gap (per-city dispatch_state -> per_city/<city>/courier_tiers.json) removes the mislabel hazard and city 2 starts empty/std. Net: this is a data/config + tenancy item, not a code-edit blocker. Per-city config key: per_city/<city>/courier_tiers.json (+ optional tier_ground_truth_seed.json), empty for a new city. Bootstrap: cold-start every courier=std (works day 1); learn gold/slow + caps over ~30-60 days from delivery data via the existing r04_apply pipeline + panel owner overrides - no code edits.

title: kurier_ids / kurier_piny / courier_tiers are single GLOBAL files (no per-city namespace)
evidence: Hardcoded absolute paths: KURIER_IDS="/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/kurier_ids.json", KURIER_PINY=...kurier_piny.json, COURIER_TIERS_PATH=...courier_tiers.json. add_new_courier() writes these 4 files atomically as the ONE roster. kurier_ids.json has 105 entries, kurier_piny.json 50 entries — all Białystok.
what_is_coupled: There is exactly one roster namespace. A second city writing couriers would collide cid/alias/name keys with Białystok couriers in the same files; resolve_cid / _load_courier_tiers / dispatchable_fleet would mix two cities' fleets into one dispatch pool.
proposed_config_key: dispatch_state path must become per-city: dispatch_state/<city>/{kurier_ids,kurier_piny,courier_tiers}.json driven by a CITY/TENANT env or config root
bootstrap_note: external setup: provision an empty per-city dispatch_state dir; add_new_courier already creates entries from scratch so a fresh city starts with an empty roster and fills via panel/auto-pairing.

verification.reasoning:
Literal facts CONFIRMED (not refuted): roster paths are hardcoded module-level constants to a single un-namespaced global dir. courier_admin.py:21-24 defines KURIER_IDS/KURIER_PINY/COURIER_TIERS as absolute /root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/*.json; identical constants are re-declared in courier_resolver.py:70/72 + COURIER_TIERS_PATH:377, new_courier_pairing.py:55-57, courier_ranking.py:30. add_new_courier writes them atomically as the ONE roster. Contents verified: kurier_ids=105, kurier_piny=50, courier_tiers=56, all Bialystok. NO city/tenant namespace exists: the only city env is ZIOMEK_DEFAULT_CITY (dispatch_pipeline.py:60, V3.29 'multi-tenant' comment), and it feeds ONLY geocoding/district resolution (lines 837/941), never the roster paths. So under SHARED-PROCESS multi-tenancy two cities WOULD collide on cid/alias keys and dispatchable_fleet would mix fleets - that part of the claim stands.

WHY P0 IS OVERSTATED -> P1: (1) The P0 bar is 'blocks even STANDING UP a 2nd city.' It does not. The paths are absolute and identical on every machine, so a franchise deployed as a separate instance/VM/filesystem root runs these exact constants unchanged with zero code edits - each box gets its own dispatch_state, no collision. The collision only manifests under single-process/shared-filesystem tenancy, which is a scale/quality concern, not a stand-up blocker. (2) The '105/50/56 all Bialystok' observation is expected per-city DATA, not code coupling - a new city bootstraps an empty roster naturally via its own gastro panel + auto-pairing; nothing to 'fix' there. (3) The proposed fix granularity (4 per-file keys) is wrong: these are just 3 of 92 distinct hardcoded dispatch_state/*.json paths (flags, plans, GPS, shadow logs, locks, pending_pool...). The codebase ALREADY has the correct mechanism - common.py:16 FLAGS_PATH = os.environ.get('DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH') or default - proving env-driven state roots are an established pattern, just not applied to the roster files.

CORRECT CONFIG KEY: a single DISPATCH_STATE_ROOT (or CITY_STATE_ROOT) env, e.g. /root/.openclaw/state/<city>/, that all 92 dispatch_state paths derive from, rather than per-file keys. Bootstrap: cold-start = point a new deployment's DISPATCH_STATE_ROOT at a fresh empty dir; roster fills itself from the new city's gastro panel via new_courier_pairing (no learned-from-data needed for the files themselves). NET: genuine single-tenant coupling and worth fixing for clean multi-city scale, but it does not block standing up city #2 as a separate deployment, so P1 not P0.

title: 4-digit courier PIN space is global, will collide across cities
evidence: _generate_unique_pin(existing_pins) draws f"{secrets.randbelow(9000)+1000:04d}" checking uniqueness only against the single kurier_piny.json (50 PINs now). PIN is the courier-app login credential (kurier_piny.json = {PIN: alias}).
what_is_coupled: PINs are unique only within the one global file. Two cities sharing the PIN namespace risk a courier in city B logging into city A's app context; 4-digit space (9000) also shrinks if pooled. PIN->alias resolution is global.
proposed_config_key: per-city PIN namespace (kurier_piny scoped per dispatch_state/<city>/) OR city-prefixed token; courier-app auth must be city-aware
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city gets its own kurier_piny.json so the 9000-PIN space resets per city; no learning needed.

verification.reasoning: Mechanics confirmed but P0 is wrong. courier_admin.py:39-44 draws 4-digit PINs (space 9000, 1000-9999), 100 retries, checking uniqueness only against the single global file (courier_admin.py:84,97); KURIER_PINY is a hardcoded absolute constant (courier_admin.py:22), not env-driven; gps_server.py:90-100 (_resolve_pin) confirms PIN is the courier-app login credential (unknown PIN to 401). However the finding's premise that two cities share the PIN namespace is an ASSUMED architecture, not a fact. Like all of dispatch_state/, kurier_piny.json is global single-tenant; the natural franchise model is one instance / one dispatch_state per city, under which each city gets its own kurier_piny.json with an independent 9000-PIN namespace, and city B's courier-app authenticates against city B's backend URL, so cross-city login is physically impossible and PINs are auto-isolated (effectively NOT-A-BLOCKER under that model). It does NOT block standing up a 2nd city (the P0 bar): a second instance mints PINs identically. The space-shrinks-if-pooled worry is overstated: even ~5 cities x 50 couriers is ~250 of 9000, handled by the 100-retry collision loop. The legitimate kernel is a single-tenant-assumption (hardcoded global file path + global uniqueness scope + global PIN-to-alias resolution) that only bites IF the franchise deliberately chooses a shared single-process multi-tenant backend, where scoping the path + uniqueness check + resolver is a small contained change = P2 at most. Proposed config key: per-city COURIER_PIN_STORE = dispatch_state/<city>/kurier_piny.json (tenant-config driven instead of the hardcoded constant), with _generate_unique_pin and _resolve_pin scoped to the active tenant; courier-app auth keyed by city backend URL. Bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed value -- new city ships an empty kurier_piny.json under its own dispatch_state path and mints PINs per-roster via the existing add_new_courier flow (no learning/data needed). Separately, 4-digit PIN entropy is a weak-auth concern, not a Bialystok coupling.

title: EXCLUDED_CIDS is a hardcoded list of named Białystok accounts
evidence: EXCLUDED_CIDS = {21 #Adrian owner, 23 #Rutcom tech, 26 #Koordynator virtual, 61 #Krystian permanent OFF, 207,284,354,426,476,498 #inactive named people}; BAR_ELJOT_COMPANY_ID=27. Consumed by new_courier_pairing.verify_courier_wired (cod_ok) + daily_accounting.
what_is_coupled: Exclusions encode this city's owner account, tech account, the virtual coordinator cid, and specific retired/inactive drivers. None apply to another city; a new city has its own owner/tech/coordinator cids and its own inactive list.
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/config: excluded_cids (with reasons), owner_cid, tech_cid, coordinator_cid, company_id
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed at city onboarding: owner enumerates owner/tech/coordinator cids + any non-courier accounts; grows over time as drivers retire (could later be data-driven from 60-day zero-delivery like the cid=61 note).

verification.reasoning:
RE-READ daily_accounting/config.py:7-20: EXCLUDED_CIDS and BAR_ELJOT_COMPANY_ID=27 are plain Python set/int LITERALS — no os.environ/getenv anywhere in the file (grep confirmed only 2 hits, both literal assignments). They encode named Bialystok accounts: cid 21=Adrian owner, 23=Rutcom tech, 26=virtual Koordynator, 61=Krystian permanent-OFF, plus 207/284/354/426/476/498 inactive named people. None of these are universal physics or shareable across cities — owner/tech/coordinator account cids and the retired-driver roster are city-specific facts. So the prior auditor's "hardcoded-value / city-coupled" classification is CORRECT, not overstated.

Consumers verified: (a) daily_accounting/main.py:97,129-130 — build_courier_iteration_list(kids, EXCLUDED_CIDS) drives who gets COD/payroll scraped; (b) new_courier_pairing.py:192,207 — cod_ok = alias in full and cid not in EXCLUDED_CIDS in verify_courier_wired(); (c) BAR_ELJOT_COMPANY_ID used in panel_scraper.py:21,184 _build_url(...company=BAR_ELJOT_COMPANY_ID) to scrape a specific company's settlement.

Why P1 not P0: the DISPATCH engine (scoring/feasibility/selection) does NOT import EXCLUDED_CIDS, so a wrong list does not block standing up dispatch for city #2 — hence not P0. But it is NOT merely cosmetic: this is the accounting/COD/payroll layer. If city #2's real courier happens to hold cid 21/23/26/207/etc., they would be silently excluded from COD scraping and payout (or owner/tech accounts get scraped as couriers), producing wrong settlement numbers. Broken payroll blocks operating a franchise city correctly — that is a scale/quality blocker = P1. (Note the same file also hardcodes SPREADSHEET_ID at line 3 — a single Bialystok Google Sheet — so the whole daily_accounting module is single-tenant; that reinforces P1 but is a separate finding.)

Not refuted, not already-configurable: there is no env/config indirection, no per-tenant annotation on these constants (unlike common.py FIRMOWE_KONTO_ADDRESS_IDS which carries a "per-tenant ready" comment). Proposed key per_city/<city>/config {excluded_cids, owner_cid, tech_cid, coordinator_cid, company_id (Bar Eljot equiv)} is the right shape. Bootstrap: cold-start fixed value — launch team enumerates the new city's owner/tech/coordinator cids + the partner-company id at onboarding; the inactive-driver list starts empty and is appended operationally over time (not learned-from-data).

title: BIALYSTOK_CENTER synthetic no-GPS courier position
evidence: BIALYSTOK_CENTER = (53.1325, 23.1688). Used ~8x as the fallback position (cs.pos = BIALYSTOK_CENTER) for couriers with no GPS and no order history (no_gps/pre_shift fallback), and in last-known-pos rescue logging.
what_is_coupled: Couriers without GPS are placed at Białystok's geographic centre so the pipeline can normalise km_to_pickup. In another city these couriers would be teleported to Białystok (hundreds of km away), corrupting feasibility/scoring for every no-GPS candidate until GPS arrives.
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/config: city_center: [lat, lon] (shared with geo lane CITY_CENTER)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: set once from the new city's centroid (single lat/lon, trivial to provide at onboarding).

verification.reasoning:
HARDCODED: confirmed. BIALYSTOK_CENTER=(53.1325,23.1688) is a bare literal, NOT read from any config/env. It is duplicated across 4 files: courier_resolver.py:110, chain_eta.py:28, bootstrap_restaurants.py:19, and a 4th copy _BIALYSTOK_CENTER_FALLBACK in dispatch_pipeline.py:132 (the (0,0)-sentinel guard). grep for getenv/os.environ/flag/CITY_CENTER/city_center near these returns nothing — there is no abstraction and no per-tenant annotation. So coupling_type=hardcoded-value is correct.

BLOCKER, but the prior auditor's mechanism is OVERSTATED for the most common case, not refuted. The claim "corrupting feasibility/scoring for every no-GPS candidate" is only partly true: dispatch_pipeline.py:5694-5717 NORMALIZES the no_gps case away — for pos_source=='no_gps' it overrides km_to_pickup=fleet_avg_km (mean of REAL fleet positions, line 5704) and travel_min/drive_min=max(15,prep) (line 5709,5714-5717). The comment at courier_resolver.py:1087-1089 confirms this is by design. So a no_gps courier's *distance/ETA* is fleet-relative and self-heals in ANY city — the literal Białystok coordinate is not used for their km. Equal-treatment logic (2026-06-24, dispatch_pipeline.py:2392-2440) further removes geographic penalty for no_gps/pre_shift.

WHY IT STILL MERITS P1 (not refuted, not P2): the normalization loop (line 5712-5717) fires ONLY for ps=='no_gps'. But BIALYSTOK_CENTER is ALSO assigned to pre_shift (courier_resolver.py:1567,1579), working_override_synthetic (:1473,1527), and post_shift_start_synthetic (:1464). Those positions are NOT normalized — they feed the literal center straight into haversine km_to_pickup and feasibility. In city #2 a pre-shift / working-override courier without GPS would be placed at a point hundreds of km from the city's restaurants → km_to_pickup ~200km → feasibility garbage for that candidate class. chain_eta.py:128,149 also falls back to BIALYSTOK_CENTER for ETA when courier_pos is None. So the coupling genuinely breaks a real (if secondary) candidate class until GPS arrives, and the 4-way literal duplication is itself a scale hazard (a launch team must change it in multiple places or refactor to a single key).

Not P0: GPS-equipped couriers (the majority on-shift) use real positions and dispatch correctly, and the dominant no_gps synthetic case is normalized to fleet-relative km, so city #2 can technically stand up and run. Not P2: the value is genuinely city-specific (a geographic centroid), MUST be set per city for pre_shift/working_override/(0,0)-fallback/chain_eta correctness, and is duplicated 4x — that's a scale/quality blocker. Proposed fix is trivial in size (one [lat,lon] per city) but mandatory: a single per_city/<city>/config.city_center key, read once and shared by all 4 sites, cold-start from a fixed value (the city's geographic center, set at launch — no data needed). Recommend the refactor also wrap the (0,0)-sentinel fallback and chain_eta fallback to that same key to kill the duplication.

title: Virtual coordinator cid=26 ('Koordynator') hardcoded
evidence: build script: `if cid == "26": continue  # Skip Koordynator`; EXCLUDED_CIDS comment '26 # Koordynator (virtual czasówki holder)'. kurier_ids.json maps 'Koordynator'->26. Panel API doc: id_kurier=26 is the czasówka holding bucket.
what_is_coupled: cid=26 is this gastro tenant's virtual coordinator/holding account. The number is tenant-specific (it's a gastro panel account id); other cities have a different coordinator cid. Hardcoding 26 in the tier builder means a new city's coordinator would be treated as a real courier.
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/config.coordinator_cid (single source, referenced by build script + EXCLUDED_CIDS + czasówka logic)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: read the new tenant's coordinator/holding cid from gastro at onboarding.

verification.reasoning:
The coupling is real and NOT already configurable. cid=26 is a bare hardcoded literal in at least 5 independent modules with zero config/env/flag indirection (my grep for os.environ/getenv/flag(/config./json.load against KOORDYNATOR/coordinator_cid returned nothing): panel_client.py:51 KOORDYNATOR_ID=26; czasowka_scheduler.py:41 KOORDYNATOR_CID="26" (plus inline :128/:133); auto_koord.py:31 KOORDYNATOR_CID=26; courier_resolver.py:791 inline str(kid)=="26"; daily_accounting/config.py:10 EXCLUDED_CIDS literal; build_v319h_courier_tiers.py:121 if cid=="26": continue.

The finding's scope is actually UNDERSTATED: it cited only the tier builder + EXCLUDED_CIDS + kurier_ids.json, but the real blast radius also spans czasowka detection (is_czasowka requires id_kurier==26, so a 2nd city with a different coordinator account detects ZERO czasowki), auto-KOORD routing (auto_koord assigns unassigned scheduled orders to cid=26, a nonexistent bucket in another tenant), panel default routing, courier-resolver fleet filtering, and daily accounting exclusion.

cid=26 is a gastro-panel courier account id; courier accounts are tenant-bound, so a 2nd franchise city would have a different coordinator cid. Worst case is a correctness landmine: if a new city's real courier happens to hold cid=26 they are silently skipped as the virtual coordinator and never dispatched.

Blocker level: P1 (not P0). Core point-to-point dispatch (feasibility/scoring/selection) does NOT reference cid=26 and stands up fine for a 2nd city, so it does not block merely standing up. But it silently breaks the entire czasowka (scheduled-pickup >=60min) feature, auto-KOORD, and accounting for the new city, degrading quality/scale until fixed. Proposed config key per_city/<city>/config.coordinator_cid is correct; should be a single source consumed by all 6 sites, NOT just the build script. Bootstrap: cold-start fixed value, read once from the new tenant's gastro panel account list (the Koordynator account id), no learning needed.

title: Tier->bag-cap matrices use Białystok peak windows + calibrated caps
evidence: BUG4_TIER_CAP_MATRIX={'gold':{off_peak:4,normal:4,peak:6},...}; HARD_TIER_BAG_CAP={'gold':6,'std+':6,'std':5,'slow':4,'new':4} 'Empiria (breach<20%, 8931 dowozów)'; bug4_pora_now peak = 11<=h<14 or 17<=h<20 (Warsaw).
what_is_coupled: The tier LABELS (gold/std+/std/slow/new) are universal, but the cap NUMBERS are calibrated from 8931 Białystok deliveries / 40k waves, and the peak/off-peak split (11-14, 17-20) is Białystok's demand curve. Another city's lunch/dinner peaks and per-tier achievable bag sizes differ, so caps would be mis-tuned (degraded, not broken — defaults catch only pathology).
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/calibration: tier_bag_cap_matrix, hard_tier_bag_cap, peak_windows (shared with peaks lane)
bootstrap_note: cold-start: ship Białystok values as a universal default (they only reject pathological 7+ bags); learned-from-data over ~30-60 days: re-derive per-tier p90 caps + peak windows from the new city's delivery log.

verification.reasoning: Re-read common.py:1219-1251 and confirmed BUG4_TIER_CAP_MATRIX, HARD_TIER_BAG_CAP, HARD_TIER_BAG_CAP_DEFAULT are literal Python dicts and the peak split (11<=h<14 or 17<=h<20) is a literal in bug4_pora_now — NOT env/config-driven (only the ENABLE_* toggles are env-overridable). No per-city/tenant config JSON dir exists. Consumers are live-coupled: dispatch_pipeline.py:4914 (SOFT penalty, default ON) and feasibility_v2.py:461 (HARD reject, default OFF). Tier LABELS are universal but the cap NUMBERS are Białystok-calibrated (comments: '40k waves', '8931 dowozów'), and the peak windows are Białystok's demand curve. Confirmed a second, independent, also-Białystok-specific peak concept right above (get_time_bucket / FALLBACK_BASE_SPEEDS_KMH common.py:455-486, commented 'peak korkowy Białegostoku', traffic-peak 15-19 vs demand-peak 11-14/17-20) — so 'peak_windows' is genuinely per-city and spans two lanes. Not refuted and not overstated: the coupling is real and hardcoded. title: restaurant_coords.json is a single global file of Białystok restaurant geocodes consumed by the whole pipeline
evidence: bootstrap_restaurants.py:17 OUT = /root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/restaurant_coords.json — one path, no city in it; read by geocoding.py, dispatch_pipeline.py, panel_watcher.py, insertion_anchor.py, bag_state.py (grep restaurant_coords).
what_is_coupled: The authoritative restaurant pickup-coordinate store is a single global JSON keyed by Białystok address_id. The dispatch engine cannot place a pickup without it. There is no per-city variant, so a second city would overwrite / collide with Białystok coords in the same file.
proposed_config_key: per_city/<city>/restaurant_coords.json + dispatch_state namespaced per tenant (dispatch_state/<city>/)
bootstrap_note: learned-from-data: run bootstrap_restaurants.py --write against the new city's panel address export once; ongoing geocodes accumulate per-city. Requires the global dispatch_state to be namespaced first (tenancy prerequisite).

verification.reasoning:
RE-READ confirms the finding's mechanics, and the P0 holds — but with an important reframe of WHAT is coupled.

WHAT IS REALLY COUPLED (verified literals, not config):
- geocoding.py:37 `RESTAURANT_CACHE_PATH = Path("/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/restaurant_coords.json")` — bare literal, no env override.
- panel_watcher.py:83 `_COORDS_PATH = "/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/restaurant_coords.json"` — separate literal (hot-reload by mtime, keyed by address_id → (lat,lng)).
- bootstrap_restaurants.py:17 `OUT = Path(".../dispatch_state/restaurant_coords.json")` — writer side, also literal; geocode() "sam dokleja ', Białystok, Polska'" (bootstrap_restaurants.py:11) so even the geocoder that POPULATES the file hardcodes the city suffix.
- dispatch_pipeline.py:832, insertion_anchor.py:114-116, bag_state.py:135 all consume the same global path/shape; plus tools/obj_harness.py:40, tools/osrm_fallback_smoke.py:29 and ~6 eod_drafts harnesses repeat the literal. So the path is duplicated, not centralized — there is no single DISPATCH_STATE_DIR seam.

ADVERSARIAL CHECK — is it "already configurable" or "overstated"?
- NOT already config/env-driven: I grepped all usages; every one is a literal string. There is no DISPATCH_STATE_DIR / tenant token. broadcast_handlers.py:65 has a scope="restaurant_coords" CONFIG_RELOAD hook, but that only triggers re-read of the SAME global file — it is per-tenant-aware in name only, not in path.
- The DATA being Białystok is expected (every city has its own restaurant set; coords are data, not code), so "hardcoded Białystok values" would be a mislabel. BUT the real coupling is structural single-tenancy: one global path + keyed by panel-global address_id (single gastro.nadajesz.pl). Two cities sharing this deployment would collide in the same file and the same id space. That genuinely blocks standing up city #2 in the same instance.
- Could two cities SHARE it? No — it is the pickup-coordinate authority; a missing/foreign entry yields pickup_coords=None (see tests/test_coord_poison_guard.py) and the engine cannot place a pickup. Not shareable.

WHY STILL P0 (not P1): restaurant_coords.json is a member of the broader dispatch_state/ global namespace (alongside geocode_cache.json, courier_last_pos.json, flags.json-adjacent state). The known-anchors note already classifies dispatch_state/ as GLOBAL single-tenant. Without namespacing this state dir, you cannot even START city #2 alongside Białystok. The cheapest tenancy architecture (one process per city) still requires this literal to become a configurable base dir — which today means editing ~5 core modules + harnesses. So it is correctly a stand-up blocker.

CORRECTED coupling_type: single-tenant-assumption (namespace/path), not "hardcoded-value" (the coords themselves are legitimate per-city data). Proposed key is right: introduce DISPATCH_STATE_DIR (env, default current path for back-compat) resolved once in common.py, with per-city base e.g. dispatch_state/<city>/restaurant_coords.json; bootstrap_restaurants.py must also parameterize the geocode city suffix (currently forced ", Białystok, Polska"). Bootstrap_note: cold-start = run bootstrap_restaurants.py --write per city after pulling that city's panel restaurant addresses (data task, no learning window needed); ongoing = panel_watcher mtime hot-reload already handles updates per-file once the path is namespaced.

title: bootstrap_restaurants.py hardcodes BIALYSTOK_CENTER and 15km outlier radius for restaurant validation
evidence: BIALYSTOK_CENTER = (53.1325, 23.1688); OUTLIER_KM = 15.0. validate() at :163 computes d_center = haversine_km((lat,lng), BIALYSTOK_CENTER); >15km → outlier flagged ('sprawdź czy to nie Supraśl/Wasilków'). Literal constants, no env/config.
what_is_coupled: The onboarding/validation tool measures every restaurant's distance from Białystok center and rejects/flags anything >15km. Run for another city, every legitimate restaurant becomes a flagged outlier (it's >15km from Białystok), making the bootstrap report useless and possibly blocking --write.
proposed_config_key: per_city config: city_center=[lat,lon], service_radius_km (reuse same value as geocode bbox / dispatch service area)

verification.reasoning:
The hardcode is REAL: bootstrap_restaurants.py:19-20 are literal constants (BIALYSTOK_CENTER = (53.1325, 23.1688); OUTLIER_KM = 15.0) with no env/config sourcing — confirmed by reading the file top. validate() at :163-165 does compute d_center against BIALYSTOK_CENTER and appends to `outliers` when >15km. So coupling_type=hardcoded-value, per-city (not universal), is accurate.

But the blocker claim (P1, "possibly blocking --write, makes bootstrap useless") is OVERSTATED on two counts:

1. Outliers do NOT gate --write. I traced validate() -> report() -> main(). At :246 `blocking = bool(hard_flagged) or bool(soft_flagged)` — only HARD/SOFT coordinate duplicates block. At :272 `if blocking and not args.force: sys.exit(1)`. Outliers (and low_accuracy) are PRINT-ONLY informational warnings (:235-238 "sprawdź czy to nie Supraśl/Wasilków"). Running for city #2, every restaurant gets listed under the orange OUTLIERS section, but the write at :278-281 still proceeds and restaurant_coords.json is produced. So it does not block standing up a 2nd city's coords — the P0/P1 "blocks" framing is wrong.

2. This is an OFFLINE one-time onboarding/bootstrap tool (dry-run report + --write), run by a human during city setup, not the live dispatch engine. Its degradation for city #2 is: the outlier sanity-check becomes pure noise (legit outliers like a town genuinely outside the service area can no longer be distinguished from normal restaurants), so the validation loses value. That degrades onboarding QA quality -> P2, not P1.

Note for the launch team: the SHARPER Białystok coupling in this same file is line 11 — `from dispatch_v2.geocoding import geocode  # sam dokleja ", Białystok, Polska"`. That city-suffix injection in geocoding.py is what would actually mis-geocode another city's addresses, and is a separate (and more severe) finding than the center/radius constants here. The center+radius themselves are trivially configurable (2 literals).

Proposed per-city config key: city.center=[lat,lon] + city.service_radius_km (reuse for geocode bbox / dispatch service area). Bootstrap_note: cold-start FIXED value — set from the city's geographic center coordinate and a chosen service radius at onboarding; no learned data needed (purely a geometric sanity bound for the validation report).

title: geocoding falls back to literal 'Białystok' and Białystok service bbox as defaults
evidence: geocoding.py:447 `return "Białystok"  # legacy kill-switch`; :515 query `f"{address}, {effective_city}, Polska"`; _bbox_config default (common.py:152 fallback) returns (52.85,53.35,22.85,23.45) = Białystok+28km. common.py:907-910 GEOCODE_BBOX_* ARE env-overridable but default Białystok. bootstrap comment line 11: geocode 'sam dokleja ", Białystok, Polska"'.
what_is_coupled: When CITY_AWARE_GEOCODING is off (kill-switch) or city missing, geocoder appends 'Białystok'; the bbox poison-guard rejects anything outside the Białystok box. CITY_AWARE path (default) takes city from panel so it's partly tenant-ready, but every default and the safety geofence is Białystok. A new city must set bbox env vars or its geocodes get bbox_reject'd.
proposed_config_key: per_city config: default_city_name (str), service_bbox=[lat_min,lat_max,lon_min,lon_max]; wire GEOCODE_BBOX_* + the literal :447 fallback to active-city config instead of env-with-Białystok-default
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: set default_city_name + service_bbox (4 numbers around the city) at onboarding. Already env-pluggable for bbox; only the :447 literal and geocode city-append need de-hardcoding.

verification.reasoning:
The finding bundles three things; re-reading the cited code shows the actual coupling is much weaker than P1.

1) geocoding.py:447 `return "Białystok"` — verified literal, BUT it is unreachable in normal operation. `_effective_city` (geocoding.py:436-448) returns the panel-supplied city if present; only when city is missing does it consult `CITY_AWARE_GEOCODING`, which defaults to True (common.py:864). With the flag True (the live default) it returns None (fail-loud), NOT "Białystok". The literal is reached only via the legacy kill-switch (flag forced False). So in the active code path the city comes from data (panel delivery_city), and the Google query `f"{address}, {effective_city}, Polska"` (geocoding.py:515) is already tenant-dynamic except the country "Polska" (fine for in-country franchise). This sub-claim is overstated/near-dead.

2) bbox geofence (common.py:907-911, _bbox_config geocoding.py:140-152) — verified the defaults ARE Białystok (52.85/53.35/22.85/23.45), but all four are `os.environ.get(..., default)` overridable, and the source comment at common.py:909 explicitly states "Multi-tenant Warsaw: bbox env-overridable per deploy." This is already-configurable, per-tenant-anticipated. A new city sets GEOCODE_BBOX_* + ENABLE_GEOCODE_BBOX_GUARD as a launch-checklist config step — no code edit. Under a process-per-city / per-deploy tenancy model (which the global env-var design assumes) this works cleanly.

3) The only residual risk is operational: if a launch team stands up city #2 and forgets the 4 bbox env vars, every out-of-box geocode gets bbox_reject'd → no dispatch. That is real but is a config-omission failure mode, not a code coupling. It degrades a misconfigured launch, hence P2, not P1, and not P0 (a correctly-configured second city stands up fine).

Net: the geofence + default city are genuine per-city settings but already env-driven, and the hardcoded "Białystok" literal sits behind a default-off kill-switch. The proposed improvement (consolidate scattered GEOCODE_BBOX_* env + the :447 fallback into one per_city config block: default_city_name + service_bbox, defaulting to None/fail-loud rather than Białystok) is a worthwhile franchise-ergonomics/safety hardening, but it is a P2 cleanup, not a P1 scale blocker.

title: OSRM is a single hardcoded localhost instance loaded only with the Podlaskie (Białystok) map extract

what_is_coupled: Every drive-time/distance computation in the engine (feasibility, scoring, plan, chain_eta, table matrix) goes to one OSRM holding only Podlaskie roads. A city outside Podlaskie has no road graph: OSRM either returns code!=Ok (→ haversine fallback, ~20% less accurate, kills routing quality) or snaps the point to the nearest Podlaskie edge and returns a phantom long route. The coord-guard (#routing-bialystok-bbox-poison-guard) actually treats every other-city coordinate as poison, so a 2nd city is unroutable.
proposed_config_key: city.osrm.base_url (per-city, e.g. http://osrm-<city>:5000) + city.osrm.extract (regional .osm.pbf to build); read OSRM_BASE from env/per-city config not a literal
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed per city: download the city's voivodeship .osm.pbf (Geofabrik), run osrm-extract/partition/customize, start a dedicated container, set city.osrm.base_url. No learning needed; pure setup. Decide tenancy: one container per city vs one multi-region extract behind a shared instance.

verification.reasoning: All three claims verified. osrm_client.py:43 OSRM_BASE is the literal string http://localhost:5001, a bare module constant with no env/config override (grep shows os.environ never touches it; flag() imports cover only the traffic multiplier and coord guard, not the base URL). On disk /root/osrm/ holds ONLY podlaskie-latest.osrm.* (Podlaskie voivodeship extract, Apr-11 build); docker ps confirms one osrm-server bound to 5001. The unroutable claim is correct and is compounded by a SECOND coupling: common.py:431-432 BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT=(52.6,53.7)/LON=(22.3,24.1) and coords_in_bialystok_bbox (common.py:435) reject any out-of-box coord; in osrm_client.py route():559-563 returns the invalid-coord sentinel WITHOUT calling OSRM, and table():645-655 remaps out-of-bbox points to _BBOX_CENTER=(53.1325,23.1688) (osrm_client.py:519). A city outside the box never reaches OSRM at all; every coordinate is poison-sentinel-ed or snapped to Bialystok center. Nothing here is config-driven: base URL, on-disk extract, and bbox bounds are all literals. Two cities could share this infra only if co-located in the same regional extract AND inside the bbox window; franchise to other cities is impossible without parameterizing all three. P0 confirmed: no road graph plus poison-guard rejection means the engine cannot compute a single valid drive-time for city #2, blocking even standing it up. Fix is mechanically simple (env/config the base, build a per-city extract, parameterize bbox/center) but the coupling is absolute, so it remains a hard P0 launch prerequisite. Proposed keys: city.osrm.base_url + city.osrm.extract + city.bbox (replacing BIALYSTOK_BBOX_* and _BBOX_CENTER). Bootstrap: fixed values per city at launch (download regional .osm.pbf, build extract, set bbox from city bounds); no data-learning needed.

title: OSRM coord-guard bbox (BIALYSTOK_BBOX) rejects any non-Białystok coordinate as poison
evidence: common.py:431-432 `BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT = (52.6, 53.7)` / `BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LON = (22.3, 24.1)` — literal tuples. coords_in_bialystok_bbox() used in osrm_client.route() (l.559) and table() (l.646) under ENABLE_OSRM_COORD_GUARD to substitute/sentinel any coord outside this box.
what_is_coupled: This ~±55km metropolitan box is the Białystok poison filter. For another city, legitimate restaurant/customer coords fall outside this box → route() returns coord_invalid sentinel and table() overwrites cells with infeasible sentinels → 2nd city is entirely infeasible even if OSRM had its map.
