verification.reasoning: CONFIRMED hardcoded: common.py:431-432 define BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LAT=(52.6,53.7) / BIALYSTOK_BBOX_LON=(22.3,24.1) as literal tuples with no os.environ read. coords_in_bialystok_bbox() (common.py:435-450) gates them, and osrm_client.route() (l.559-560) + table() (l.645-646) substitute any out-of-box coord with the OSRM_INVALID_COORD_SENTINEL_MIN=9999 sentinel under ENABLE_OSRM_COORD_GUARD (default ON, common.py:2591). A 2nd city's legitimate coords fall outside this ~Bialystok-metro box, so every route/table cell is poisoned as infeasible. The coupling is genuine AND an oversight: the SIBLING geocode bbox (common.py:906-910 GEOCODE_BBOX_*) is ALREADY env-overridable with an explicit author comment 'Multi-tenant Warsaw: bbox env-overridable per deploy' — the authors completed half the multi-tenant work and left the metropolitan bbox as literals. NOT universal physics (it is a city geographic box), NOT shareable (each city has different lat/lon). However NOT P0: ENABLE_OSRM_COORD_GUARD is a kill-switch env flag (common.py:2591), so a 2nd city CAN technically stand up by setting it to 0; routes then compute fine if OSRM has the map. Disabling the guard globally only removes the (0,0)/phantom-poison defense (Lekcja #140) — a safety degradation, not a standup blocker. To run city #2 with the poison net intact requires a code edit or env-driving the tuples. That blocks safe scale/quality => P1, matching the auditor's claim. Fix is trivial: mirror the existing GEOCODE_BBOX_* env pattern with METRO_BBOX_LAT_MIN/MAX + METRO_BBOX_LON_MIN/MAX, preserving the test_geo_bbox_consistency invariant GEOCODE_BBOX subset of METRO per city. Bootstrap: cold-start fixed value from the new city's metro envelope (~+/-55km around city center), no learning needed.

title: Geocode caches and neg-cache live in one GLOBAL single-tenant dispatch_state dir
evidence: geocoding.py:36-41 `CACHE_PATH = .../dispatch_state/geocode_cache.json`, `RESTAURANT_CACHE_PATH = .../restaurant_coords.json`, `NEG_CACHE_PATH = .../geocode_neg_cache.json` — fixed absolute paths, one per process. The finding bundles three sub-claims of very different severity, and the P1 framing inflates it.

1) ADDRESS cache (geocode_cache.json) collision — REFUTED. `_normalize` (geocoding.py:251, esp. lines 269-272) appends ", <city>" to every key, and `_effective_city`/`geocode` (436-470) require a real city (fail-loud None when CITY_AWARE_GEOCODING=True, the default; legacy hardcode "Białystok" only on the off kill-switch at line 447). So address keys are already city-namespaced. Two cities could even SHARE this cache safely and beneficially. Not a blocker.

2) The neg-cache (geocode_neg_cache.json) uses the same `_normalize(address, city)` key (comment line 39-40), so it inherits the same city-qualification. Safe.

3) RESTAURANT cache (restaurant_coords.json) — the one genuine defect. Key is bare lowercased name: `key = name.strip().lower()` (geocoding.py:641), NOT city-qualified. Identical restaurant names across cities WOULD collide. But this is a real hazard ONLY if two cities run in ONE process against ONE state dir. It is a one-line fix (qualify key with city). P3 / defensive.

On deployment model: the absolute paths (lines 36-41) and the global dispatch_state are part of the system-wide single-tenant assumption that ALSO covers flags.json, courier_plans.json, orders_state.json and the single gastro.nadajesz.pl panel. The natural franchise topology — mirroring that single-tenancy — is a container/VM per city, where each city gets its own /root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state. In that model all three caches are isolated automatically, the restaurant-name collision never fires (each cache holds only that city's restaurants), and `_stats` is per-process. So the genuinely load-bearing architectural decision ("dispatch_state is global single-tenant; pick container-per-city or namespace state_dir") is a SEPARATE, broader finding — attributing P1 specifically to the geocode caches double-counts it. The geocode-specific residue is just the restaurant-key qualification (P3).

Proposed config still useful: if cities are ever co-located in one process, introduce city.state_dir (namespace dispatch_state/<city>/, make the three Path literals derive from a configurable workspace root) AND qualify the restaurant cache key with city. title: Panel order id (zid) used as global key — two panels can collide
evidence: panel_html_parser/panel_client extract numeric panel ids (regex e.g. 46\d{4}/47XXXX history at panel_client.py:90-92) and use them directly as orders_state keys; no city prefix. Each gastro panel has its own incrementing id space, so two cities WILL eventually emit the same numeric zid.
what_is_coupled: If two cities ever share one orders_state.json / events.db, identical zids overwrite each other (one city's order 483504 clobbers the other's). Safe ONLY if state is partitioned per city (see panel-global-state-config-singleton).
proposed_config_key: n/a if process-per-city (state_dir partition removes collision); else order keys must be namespaced tenant_id:zid
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: partition state per city; no learning needed

verification.reasoning:
The literal observation is correct: zid is used un-namespaced as the orders_state key. v2 parser extracts a bare numeric string (panel_html_parser.py:87, regex `id:\s*(\d{5,7})`); v1 legacy was `id:\s*(46\d{4})` (panel_html_parser.py:353); state_machine.py keys orders_state.json by that raw zid with no city prefix. But the P1 framing is overstated for three reasons.

(1) The collision is conditional, not inherent. Each Ziomek instance polls exactly ONE panel: panel_client.py:36 BASE_URL='https://www.gastro.nadajesz.pl' is a single hardcoded source, and panel_watcher fetches that one panel's HTML into one orders_state.json. So a process only ever ingests zids from its own city; the keyspace is single-source by construction. Two zids collide ONLY if two cities' panels are merged into one shared orders_state.json/events.db. The finding is therefore not independent - it collapses into the separate panel-global-state-config-singleton decision, and the finding text itself concedes 'Safe ONLY if state is partitioned per city'.

(2) Partitioning already exists. state_machine.py:_state_path honors the DISPATCH_STATE_DIR env override (added Faza 2b 2026-05-18) and otherwise reads load_config()['paths']['orders_state'] - a config value, not a literal. Separate-state-per-city is achievable today with zero code edit, and a container/host-per-city deployment (the natural model, since flags.json + dispatch_state/ are global singletons) makes each city's filesystem physically distinct. Under process-per-city the collision can never occur at runtime.

(3) The only genuine residual risk is at a cross-city DATA-AGGREGATION layer: if the franchise later pools every city's shadow_decisions.jsonl/events into one ML training warehouse (the MOAT depends on learned data), raw zids from different cities would alias during dedup/joins. That is real but minor, offline, and solved trivially by stamping a tenant_id/city column at ingest - hence P3, not a runtime-overwrite P1.

Proposed config key: none needed for runtime (rely on process-per-city + DISPATCH_STATE_DIR/config paths). For the analytics layer, add tenant_id/city_id stamped at log/event ingestion so the composite key becomes (city_id, zid). Bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed value - assign each city a static city_id at provisioning and inject it into the log/event writers; no learning required.

title: Single courier roster source (/admin2017/list-users) with no tenant scoping
evidence: LIST_USERS_PATH = "/admin2017/list-users"; fetch_full_roster/fetch_active_roster use a single module-level _cache and the single panel_client opener. parse_list_users keys couriers by gastro cid with no city dimension. verification.reasoning:
The finding conflates a universal module with a genuine-but-misattributed coupling. panel_roster.py is per-city-clean: zero "bialystok"/"city"/"miasto" literals (grep empty), the parser (typ_user='kurier', activeKurier(cid) btn-success, edit-user fallback) is platform-universal, it is fail-open ([] on any error), and it keys by gastro cid which is unambiguous WITHIN one panel. The module never hardcodes a roster — it scrapes whatever panel it is pointed at, so city #2's roster comes for FREE with no code change.

The single _cache (panel_roster.py:40) and "serves whichever panel was fetched last" concern only bites in a multi-tenant-single-process design. But the system is architecturally forced into process-per-city: flags.json and dispatch_state/ are global single-tenant, and the panel pointer panel_client.BASE_URL = "https://www.gastro.nadajesz.pl" (panel_client.py:36) is a module-level literal, not env-read (contrast ENABLE_PANEL_BG_REFRESH/USE_V2_PARSER on lines 83/92 which DO read os.environ). Under process-per-city the module-level _cache is per-process and correct, and cid-space partitioning is automatic. So the roster's "no tenant scoping" is a non-issue under the only viable tenancy model.

The REAL per-city lever is therefore not in panel_roster at all — it is panel_client.BASE_URL (hardcoded literal) plus the single credentials path PANEL_ENV = .secrets/panel.env (panel_client.py:37). Credentials are already externalized to a file (half-ready); the URL is the one literal that must become per-process config. That is a small, mechanical change (one literal -> env/per-tenant secrets dir), not a roster redesign. Hence the roster finding itself is overstated: it needs no per-city code; merits at most P2 because making BASE_URL+creds-path per-process config is required work but is trivial and belongs to the panel-endpoint finding, not the roster. Config key: PANEL_BASE_URL + PANEL_SECRETS_DIR per process (cold-start: set both at city launch from the franchise panel onboarding; roster then self-populates by scraping list-users — no learned data needed).

A universal-defaults+overrides LAYER already exists: decision_flag/flag resolve flags.json -> module constant -> False, so module constants are the shared universal layer and a new city's flags.json only needs to carry its overrides plus its own identities. The Białystok identities ARE in the file (flags.json:27 SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID, :36 BARTEK_USER_ID) but that is exactly the per-city data that belongs in the per-city flag file, so it is not a blocker. None of these block standing up or running a second city's live dispatch. Per-city config key: tenancy.flags_path already realized as DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH; recommend extending the same env to the tools/ family and extracting notify identities into a per-city notify.json.

evidence: SCHEMA_PATH=dispatch_state/r04_schema.json (gates read from file, not hardcoded); 30d window; metrics from events.db. Gate thresholds (peak_deliveries, speed) per-tier.
what_is_coupled: Graduation thresholds reflect Białystok throughput norms but are already in an external JSON, so per-city is a config swap not a code edit. 30d window is reasonable universal default.
proposed_config_key: per_city.fleet.r04_schema_path
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: copy Białystok schema as starting gates; refine per city as throughput data accrues.

title: Shared dispatch_state dir hardcoded as absolute literal in 433 places (state root not a single variable)
evidence: `grep -rno '/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state' --include=*.py` => 433 occurrences across 243 files. e.g. dispatch_pipeline.py:2167 `LOADGOV_ORDERS_STATE_PATH = "/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/orders_state.json"`; dispatch_pipeline.py:73 `"RULE_WEIGHTS_PATH", "/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/rule_weights.json"`. Only 15 files honor an env override (DISPATCH_STATE_DIR); the other ~228 are pure literals.
what_is_coupled: Every city writes/reads the SAME orders_state.json, courier_plans.json, courier_tiers.json, shadow logs, calibration logs and sqlite DBs. Two cities sharing this dir would corrupt each other's fleet state. There is no single STATE_ROOT variable to repoint; you would have to edit 200+ files.
proposed_config_key: STATE_ROOT env/config (e.g. DISPATCH_STATE_DIR=/var/ziomek/<city>/state) consumed by ALL path builders via one common.py helper state_path(name)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city gets an empty state dir; engine populates it from first tick. Requires refactor: introduce common.state_path() and replace the 433 literals.

title: flags.json is a single global file (env-overridable hook exists but content is Białystok-specific)
evidence: common.py:16 `FLAGS_PATH = Path(os.environ.get("DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH") or (SCRIPTS_DIR / "flags.json"))` — path IS env-overridable. BUT flags.json embeds per-city values: `SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID: -5149910559`, `BARTEK_USER_ID: 8753482870`, `KONIEC_AUTHORIZED_USER_IDS: [8765130486, 8753482870]`.
what_is_coupled: All ~200 feature flags AND per-city telegram/user IDs live in one file shared by every process. The DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH hook makes per-city flag files POSSIBLE, but nothing sets it today and the engine assumes one global flags.json. Mixing universal feature toggles with per-city operator IDs in one file means a new city cannot reuse the flag set without inheriting Białystok chat/user IDs.
proposed_config_key: DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH=/var/ziomek/<city>/flags.json per city; split universal feature toggles (global default) from per-city overlay (chat_ids, authorized_users)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: copy the universal flag template, override the per-city chat/user IDs. Hook already exists; just needs per-city files + the IDs externalized.

title: config.json (timezone, telegram IDs, paths, scoring weights) is a single fixed file, NOT env-overridable
evidence: common.py:12 `CONFIG_PATH = SCRIPTS_DIR / "config.json"` — NO env override (asymmetric with FLAGS_PATH on line 16). config.json holds `timezone:"Europe/Warsaw"`, `telegram.admin_id:"-5149910559"`, `telegram.personal_admin_id:"8765130486"`, `paths.orders_state:"/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/orders_state.json"`, scoring weights. state_machine.py:181 reads canonical path via `load_config()["paths"]["orders_state"]`.
what_is_coupled: The canonical orders_state path AND telegram IDs AND timezone are pinned to one file with no per-city selector. config.json is the NATURAL per-city config carrier but is currently fixed-path single-tenant; you cannot run city #2 without it pointing at city #2's state and chat.
proposed_config_key: DISPATCH_CONFIG_PATH env (mirror FLAGS_PATH pattern) selecting per-city config.json with that city's paths/timezone/telegram block
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city ships a config.json with its paths, timezone, telegram IDs. Needs code change to make CONFIG_PATH env-selectable.

title: ~60 dispatch-* / nadajesz-* systemd units are single non-templated instances
evidence: All core units are plain (not `@.service` templates): dispatch-shadow.service `ExecStart=.../python -m dispatch_v2.shadow_dispatcher`, `WorkingDirectory=/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts`; dispatch-gps.service hard-codes `ReadWritePaths=/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state`. Only `dispatch-onfailure-alert@.service` and `papu-telegram-alert@.service` use templating. No instance carries a city identifier or per-city Environment.
what_is_coupled: There is exactly ONE shadow dispatcher, ONE panel watcher, ONE gps server, ONE prune/snapshot/reconcile loop for the whole deployment. Running a 2nd city requires a 2nd full set of services with per-instance state/flags/config env. Today that means hand-cloning ~60 unit files.
proposed_config_key: Templated units `dispatch-shadow@<city>.service` with `EnvironmentFile=/etc/ziomek/<city>.env` injecting DISPATCH_STATE_DIR / DISPATCH_FLAGS_PATH / DISPATCH_CONFIG_PATH / panel creds
bootstrap_note: external setup: per-city deploy provisions an env file + enables templated instances. Depends on the state/flags/config externalization above.

title: panel_client BASE_URL hardcoded to one gastro.nadajesz.pl tenant
evidence: panel_client.py:36 `BASE_URL = "https://www.gastro.nadajesz.pl"`; login flow hits `f"{BASE_URL}/admin2017/login"`. Single set of panel creds in /root/.openclaw/workspace/.secrets/panel.env + per-courier panel_courier_*.env.
what_is_coupled: The entire order/event data source is one gastro panel account. A new city is a different gastro tenant (or different panel domain entirely) with its own login + courier IDs. Nothing parameterizes which panel a process talks to.
proposed_config_key: PANEL_BASE_URL + PANEL_CREDENTIALS_FILE per city (config.json `panel.base_url` + per-city secrets path)
bootstrap_note: external setup: each city provisions a gastro tenant + credentials before launch; set base_url/creds env per instance.

title: OSRM router hardcoded to localhost:5001 with a Białystok-only extract and city-sized cache assumption
evidence: osrm_client.py:43 `OSRM_BASE = "http://localhost:5001"`; line 44 `CACHE_TTL_SECONDS = 60*60  # ... Białystok skończony zestaw routes, 99% hit po 1h warm-up`. common.py:496 comment `Self-hosted OSRM Docker (:5001) returns FREE-FLOW road durations`.
what_is_coupled: One OSRM container loaded with the Białystok OSM extract serves all routing. The 1h cache TTL is explicitly justified by Białystok being a 'finite route set' — a larger city breaks the 99%-hit assumption. A new city needs its own OSM extract (separate container/port) or a wider national extract.
proposed_config_key: OSRM_BASE per city (e.g. http://localhost:<port> with city extract) + per-city cache sizing config
bootstrap_note: external setup: spin a per-city OSRM container with that city's OSM extract; set OSRM_BASE env per instance. Cache TTL re-evaluated for larger cities.

title: Operator telegram group/user IDs hardcoded across code AND duplicated in config.json/flags.json
evidence: `-5149910559` (Grupa ziomka) hardcoded in parser_health_endpoint.py:69 GROUP_CHAT_ID, czasowka_scheduler.py:36/38 GROUP_CHAT_ID_FALLBACK, shift_notifications/telegram_send.py:55, czasowka_proactive/evaluator.py:50, tools/reassignment_forward_shadow.py:336; ALSO config.json telegram.admin_id and flags.json SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID. Personal admin 8765130486 + Bartek 8753482870 likewise.
what_is_coupled: Every notification path targets the Białystok operator group/coordinators. The same ID is duplicated in config, flags, and >6 source files (no single source of truth), so a new city's notifications cannot be routed without editing code in multiple places.
proposed_config_key: config.json telegram.{group_chat_id, coordinator_user_ids} as the SINGLE source; remove the in-code literal fallbacks
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city sets its telegram group + coordinator user IDs at launch. Requires de-duplicating the literal fallbacks down to config.

title: daily_accounting/config.py hardcodes Białystok roster exclusions, Google Sheet ID and company ID
evidence: `SPREADSHEET_ID = "1Z5kSGUB0Tfl1TiUs5ho-ecMYJVz0-VuUctoq781OSK8"`; `EXCLUDED_CIDS = {21,23,26,61,207,284,354,426,476,498}` with per-person comments (Adrian owner, Krystian OFF od 22.04, Mykyta nie pracuje...); `BAR_ELJOT_COMPANY_ID = 27`.

bootstrap_note: learned-from-data + manual: roster excludes are curated per city as couriers are onboarded; sheet ID provisioned at setup.

title: Three global sqlite DBs (courier_api.db, events.db, fleet_analytics.db) in shared state dir
evidence: config.json paths.events_db `/root/.openclaw/workspace/dispatch_state/events.db`; ls shows courier_api.db (25MB), events.db, fleet_analytics.db all in the single shared dispatch_state dir.
what_is_coupled: Order events, courier API state and analytics are single shared databases keyed by gastro IDs from one tenant. Two cities would interleave events/couriers in the same tables.
proposed_config_key: per-city DB paths under STATE_ROOT/<city>/ (events_db, courier_api_db, fleet_analytics_db) selected via config
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city gets fresh empty DBs created on first run under its state root.

title: Inter-process file locks live in the shared state dir (serialize/collide if multi-tenant single process)
evidence: ls shows orders_state.json.lock, courier_plans.lock, courier_tiers.json.lock, coordinator_activations.json.lock, pending_pool.lock, czasowka_*.lock — all path-global in the single state dir; state_machine.py uses `_locked_write()`.
what_is_coupled: Write coordination is per-FILE in a global dir. A single multi-tenant process pointed at shared files would serialize all cities through one lock (throughput collapse) and risk cross-city writes. This is the key reason the tenancy model should be process-per-city with separate state dirs (locks then naturally isolate).
proposed_config_key: n/a — universal mechanism; isolation achieved by per-city STATE_ROOT so locks are per-city by construction
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: locks auto-created under each city's state root once STATE_ROOT is parameterized.

title: Logs directory is a single global path referenced 224 times
evidence: `grep -rno '/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/logs' --include=*.py` => 224 occurrences; e.g. osrm_client.py:46 logs to `/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/logs/dispatch.log`; systemd units StandardOutput=append:/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/logs/*.log.
what_is_coupled: All cities would interleave logs in one directory, making per-city debugging and shadow_decisions.jsonl analysis ambiguous. Degrades observability rather than blocking startup.
proposed_config_key: LOG_DIR per city under STATE_ROOT/<city>/logs (config paths.shadow_log / dispatch_log already exist, just need per-city base)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: per-city log dir created at provision time; paths derived from STATE_ROOT.

title: WARSAW timezone hardcoded in common.py; config.json timezone key is NOT consumed
evidence: common.py:357 `WARSAW = ZoneInfo("Europe/Warsaw")` used pervasively; config.json has `timezone:"Europe/Warsaw"` but grep finds NO code reading config timezone — it is dead config. Dozens of `*_HOUR_WARSAW` constants reference WARSAW.
what_is_coupled: Timezone is fixed to Europe/Warsaw. For a Polish-only franchise this is effectively universal (P3); only an international city would need it. Notably the config key exists but is ignored, so it gives false confidence of configurability.
proposed_config_key: config.json timezone ACTUALLY consumed (replace hardcoded WARSAW with load_config tz) — only needed if expanding beyond Poland
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: Polish cities keep Europe/Warsaw; wire config tz only for cross-border expansion.

title: V326_OSRM_TRAFFIC_TABLE calibrated from 42,494 Białystok deliveries, hardcoded in common.py
evidence: common.py:516 `V326_OSRM_TRAFFIC_TABLE = {...}` with header `empirical bucket SHAPE (anchor-A method, n=42,494 deliveries Nov2025-Apr2026)`; per-hour multipliers (weekday 15-17 = 1.55 etc.) and `V326_OSRM_DISTANCE_BIN_BOOST_PEAK` from a TomTom Białystok sample.
what_is_coupled: OSRM free-flow durations are corrected by a traffic curve fit to Białystok rush-hour patterns. Another city's congestion shape differs (different peak hours, district sizes). Using Białystok's table degrades ETA accuracy elsewhere — relevant to my lane because it is a hardcoded constant that must become per-city calibrated state.
proposed_config_key: per-city traffic_table loaded from STATE_ROOT/<city>/traffic_multiplier.json (learned), with a national default for cold-start
bootstrap_note: learned-from-data over ~14-30 days: cold-start uses a generic Polish-mid-city default table; recalibrate from that city's delivered legs once n>=500/bucket (same wariant-B method).

title: Entire notification stack bound to single global config.json + flags.json + dispatch_state/ (single tenant)
evidence: common.py: SCRIPTS_DIR=Path('/root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts'); CONFIG_PATH=SCRIPTS_DIR/'config.json'; FLAGS_PATH=...'flags.json'. load_config() returns one module-cached dict. telegram_approver main_async: cfg=load_config(); admin_id=str(cfg['telegram']['admin_id']); state={'admin_id':admin_id,...} — one process, one admin_id. notify_router: _STATE_DIR=Path('.../dispatch_state'); FEED_PATH/CONFIG_PATH single global files.
what_is_coupled: There is exactly one config.json, one flags.json, one dispatch_state/, and the approver/notifier/router all read these singletons. A second city cannot be served by the same process: admin_id is a single value, flags.json keys (BARTEK_USER_ID, SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID, KONIEC_AUTHORIZED_USER_IDS) are global, notify_feed.jsonl/notify_routing.json are global. Running city #2 requires either per-city process instances each pointed at a per-city config root, or a tenant dimension threaded through load_config/load_flags and every state path.
proposed_config_key: Per-city config root: e.g. CONFIG_ROOT env per systemd unit -> {city}/config.json, {city}/flags.json, {city}/dispatch_state/; OR add top-level 'tenant_id' and namespace all dispatch_state paths by tenant
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: provision a per-city config dir + dedicated systemd units (dispatch-telegram@bialystok, @city2) each with CONFIG_ROOT pointing at that city's tree

title: Coordinator Telegram group id is config-driven but the config file is a global singleton
evidence: config.json: "telegram": {"admin_id": "-5149910559", "personal_admin_id": "8765130486", "group_name": "Grupa ziomka", "bot_dispatch":"@NadajeszBot", "bot_control":"@GastroBot"}. Value is read via load_config()['telegram']['admin_id'] (NOT a literal in code) — good — but only one config.json exists.
what_is_coupled: All ~40 proposal/alert sends use state['admin_id'] = the single Białystok group -5149910559. Already externalized so a new city just sets a new value, but because config.json is global only ONE group can be active per deployment. group_name 'Grupa ziomka' is descriptive only.
proposed_config_key: telegram.admin_id (already exists) — make per-city by per-city config root; add telegram.group_name per city
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: create the city's Telegram group, add the bot, read the negative group chat id, write into that city's config.json

title: Bot tokens loaded from single global .secrets/telegram.env + assistant_telegram.env (per-city bot needed)
evidence: TELEGRAM_ENV_PATH='/root/.openclaw/workspace/.secrets/telegram.env'; env=_load_env(...); token=env.get('TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN',''). notify_router: _ASSISTANT_ENV_PATH='.../.secrets/assistant_telegram.env', token=env.get('ASSISTANT_TELEGRAM_TOKEN'). shift_notifications/telegram_send.py: TELEGRAM_SECRETS_PATH same global file.
what_is_coupled: One bot token = one BotFather bot = one bot identity. Each city needs its own dispatch bot (and optionally its own quiet/assistant bot) registered with BotFather, plus its own secrets file. Path is a single global literal so a 2nd city's token has nowhere to live without a per-city secrets dir.
proposed_config_key: Per-city secrets path: {CONFIG_ROOT}/.secrets/telegram.env + assistant_telegram.env; keys TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN / ASSISTANT_TELEGRAM_TOKEN per city
bootstrap_note: external setup: register a new bot via BotFather per city, add it to that city's group, store token in the city's secrets file (5 min, manual)

title: Authorized operator Telegram user ids hardcoded as default (Adrian + Bartek)
evidence: _KONIEC_AUTHORIZED_USER_IDS_DEFAULT = [8765130486, 8753482870]  # Adrian=8765130486; Bartek=8753482870. _authorized_user_ids() prefers flags.json key but falls back to this literal. flags.json currently has KONIEC_AUTHORIZED_USER_IDS=[8765130486, 8753482870] (same Białystok operators).
what_is_coupled: Who may issue /koniec and 'kurier przyszedł' commands. These are the personal Telegram user ids of the two Białystok operators. A new city has different humans -> different ids. Overridable via flags.json (good) but the default and the live values are Białystok-specific, and flags.json is global.
proposed_config_key: telegram.authorized_user_ids (per-city list) — relocate from flags.json default into per-city config
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: each city's operators send /start to the city bot, extract their user_id from journalctl, write the per-city list

title: Coordinator DM-routing user id (BARTEK_USER_ID) is per-city operator, lives in global flags.json
evidence: _resolve_bartek_alert_target: bartek_id=cfg.get('BARTEK_USER_ID'); enabled=bool(cfg.get('COORDINATOR_DM_ROUTING_ENABLED',False)); else group fallback state['admin_id']. flags.json: BARTEK_USER_ID=8753482870, COORDINATOR_DM_ROUTING_ENABLED=True.
what_is_coupled: no-show alerts DM the named coordinator (Bartek, id 8753482870) instead of the whole group. Variable name 'BARTEK' bakes in the Białystok coordinator's identity. Per city this is a different person; fallback is group send.
proposed_config_key: telegram.coordinator_dm_user_id + telegram.coordinator_dm_enabled (rename off the 'BARTEK' literal)
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: capture the city coordinator's Telegram user_id after /start; default disabled -> safe group fallback until set

title: ADRIAN_CHAT_ID_FALLBACK hardcoded literal in shift notifications send path
evidence: ADRIAN_CHAT_ID_FALLBACK = 8765130486. _resolve_target_chat order: flags.json SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID (=-5149910559 group) -> else ADRIAN_CHAT_ID_FALLBACK (8765130486 DM Adriana). _resolve_chat_id also defaults to this literal.
what_is_coupled: Shift-start/reminder notifications fall back to Adrian's personal DM if no group configured. The literal is the Białystok owner's chat id; a misconfigured new city would silently DM Adrian rather than the city's group/operator.
proposed_config_key: telegram.shift_notify_target_chat_id (already SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID in flags) + telegram.fallback_dm_chat_id per city; remove hardcoded literal
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: set SHIFT_NOTIFY_TARGET_CHAT_ID to the city group in that city's flags.json; per-city fallback dm

title: Coordinator virtual courier id_kurier=26 hardcoded across czasówka holding / koord assignment
evidence: auto_koord.py: KOORDYNATOR_CID = 26. czasowka_scheduler.py: KOORDYNATOR_CID = '26'; czasówka = prep>=60min AND held by Koordynator (id_kurier=26). courier_resolver.py:791: if str(kid)=='26' and not include_koordynator: (skip from fleet). project_overview confirms 'id_kurier (26 = Koordynator virtual czasówki)'.
what_is_coupled: Białystok's gastro panel happens to assign id_kurier=26 to the virtual 'Koordynator' account that holds scheduled/time-window orders. A new city's panel will create its own virtual coordinator with a DIFFERENT numeric id. Hardcoding 26 means czasówka detection, koord-redirect, and fleet exclusion all break/misfire in city #2.
proposed_config_key: panel.koordynator_cid (per-city int) read from config; replace the 26 literals
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed: read the city panel's virtual coordinator courier id once during onboarding and write it to config

title: Briefing peak-hours window hardcoded (lunch 11-14, dinner 17-20)
evidence: _PEAK_HOURS_WARSAW = frozenset(range(11,14)) | frozenset(range(17,20)); used to bucket on-time stats into peak/off in morning/evening briefing.

Polish cities are similar so cold-start default is safe, but a city with different rhythm gets mis-bucketed peak/off-peak stats. Reporting-only here (not a dispatch decision in this file).
proposed_config_key: city.peak_windows (list of hour ranges) — shared with the engine's peak definition
bootstrap_note: cold-start fixed (11-14/17-20 default), then learned-from-data over ~14-30 days of order-volume histogram per city

evidence: Help string: 'Jako <imię> używaj formy z panelu (np. Adrian Cit, Bartek O., Mateusz O).' — example courier names are Białystok roster members.

evidence: notify_router.py: WARSAW = ZoneInfo('Europe/Warsaw'). daily_briefing imports WARSAW from common. config.json already has top-level 'timezone':'Europe/Warsaw'.
